description:
Combine Yummy Yummy Pancake: Promo Boards with the Yummy Yummy Pancake base game to enjoy an even more exciting gaming experience!

What changes?

     The aim of the game when using boards changes: Be the first to complete your board with the appropriate pancakes.
     During set-up, all pancakes are put face-up on the table, so the chef gets to choose which toppings will be placed in the frying pan.


How to use the boards?

     Each player starts the game with a board and must complete it accordingly. The chocolate pancake is now a joker and can be used as any topping, but you can't move it once played on your board.
     If you get pancakes you don't need, place them face-down in the designated space. If full, you must exchange three of them with one remaining pancake from the table to add to your board.
     Small tip! For easy gameplay, use the board's face with only four tokens to match.

Description from the publisher:

In the year 635 Scottish monks founded an abbey on an island before the north coast of England, and gave it the same name as the island: Lindisfarne.

Under the leadership of Aidan, the monastery flourished and became a center of Christian art and culture. In 651 Aidan died - and the struggle for his succession begins.

In the game Lindisfarne 2 - 4 players try to become the new abbot of the monastery Lindisfarne. To this end, they have to win not only the support of their fellow-monks, but must also gain the favor of the king in order to expel the fellow-competitors.
At the same time, the players plan their actions and the order in which they are executed. Pray, read the mass for a nobleman, support the expansion of the monastery, or let another monk work for you - with each of the actions the players get closer to their goal. It may also be helpful to slander the other players or hinder them in other ways.

In principle, each player has the same actions - but the acquisition and playing of cards results in additional possibilities.
Lindisfarne is a game that combines a high degree of interaction and tactical consideration, and is therefore aimed at players, who play regularly.

Garçon! En délire!!! is a reimplementation of Garçon. It is a card game in which the players hold the role of waiters in a Café Bar. Their objective is to memorize orders to gain tips, but in order to carry them it will be necessary to judiciously use the Event Cards, to thwart the plans of the other players.

The main difference with the original game is the goofiness of the ingredient names as “Jus de limace” (slug juice), “Sodas Inconnus” (a game play around “Soldats inconnu”, ie. “Unknown soldier”) or “2 MiMoore” (read in french as Demi Moore). This renders the orders far more difficult to memorize.

The game consists of 55 cards and is suited for 3–10 players Ages 7 and up (but is this a pun about the soda drink?).

A highly replayable fantasy based game designed to mimic the feel and pace of competitive arena e-sports. Oathen uses six heroes with their own unique playstyles and skill decks, it comes with three game modes, and a dynamically changing arena that makes each match unique.

Adventure into a randomly generated game board full of traps and treasures, where you will battle it out for the title of arena champion. Players will pre-plan their turns ahead of time, studying their competitors and finding the right combination of abilities to grant them victory. Will your oathen hero unleash a flurry of blows against your target? Or will the other players play defensively to negate your attacks?

Combat carries out one ability at a time, each player playing their next chosen action. The strongest of abilities will then go on cooldown, and will be unavailable for use until a certain number of turns has passed. Plan correctly, and you could pull off amazing combos - fail, and you may be exposed to such attacks yourself.

The arena is constructed with 36 of 52 total locations, providing boosts and traps that can alter your battle, and making every game into a unique match. Oathen also comes with different game modes, including arena, capture the flag, and round table.

Choose between six Oathen champions to play as. Each one plays differently with 20 unique abilities to plan - including an ultimate ability that can turn the tides of battle. Future expansions will increase the number of heroes to 24, including defensive characters, healers, and devastating damage dealers.

Description from the publisher:

Tossers is a game where players take turns placing a marked container somewhere, choosing a spot to throw from, making up a tossing rule, and then tossing their beads 1 at a time towards the container. After the beads are tossed, the points are scored and the next player gets to move the container, choose a new spot to throw from, and make up a tossing rule.

User summary
心霊写真 (Ghost Picture) (Shinrei shashin) is a physical game that uses a mobile phone app to play. The game box includes a light frame which the player constructs. This holds the player's phone, a clear plastic screen and a small board onto which players' cards are placed.

Before playing, the player must download the free app onto their phone, and then set it so the screen does not go to sleep while playing.

The cards show different parts of the dark forest, and the players try to make their way home without being caught by a ghost. A player takes turns to place a card from their hand onto the board, so the image faces the phone. Each card includes a QR code, which the phone's camera reads. Then a ghost might be projected by the phone screen and appears hovering in the forest.

In fact, the image is on the clear plastic screen in front of the forest card, an illusion similar to the theatrical trick Pepper's Ghost.

If the player sees a ghost, they must take all the cards that have been placed in the stand. If there is no ghost, they leave their card on the stand. In ths way, cards build up for the player who gets caught.

Some cards have extra effects which cause the app to increase the odds of a ghost appearing. Or a player can swap their cards with another player and so on.

The game was produced in 2015 and sold out its initial print run. The app was made for iPhones 5 and 6, and later for Android. After 2016, there appears to be a break in development. Although published in Japanese, gameplay is language free and English rules are online.
publishers: Projection Card (プロジェクションカードバンド)

description:
The Gods are waking up from a long sleep and taking possession of the best fighters of the realm, while waiting to have recovered all their powers. You and your opponent are 2 of these fighters, like 2 Heralds of the Gods.

Damoclès is both a micro-skirmish game and a card game, in a 1vs1 mode.

The first player who inflicts 9 wounds to his opponent wins !

The two players fight one another with a clever combination of action cards. This is a double-guessing system with planning and anticipation. The cards stand for your life in miniature as well.

You may count on a god’s favor to make the difference, unless you trigger their terrible wrath…!

At your turn, choose action cards and play one simultaneously. Cards have a double-direction reading, so do the right choice before you  reveal them. Then, resolve the effects. Wounds will cause reorientations and retreats of the figures on the board game.

You lose a card from your deck for each wound. So if you lose 9 cards, you’re defeated !


Entrez dans l'arène de Damoclès.

Damoclès Kézako ?

Les Dieux se réveillent d’un long sommeil et prennent possession des meilleurs combattants du royaume en attendant d’avoir recouvré leur toute puissance pour asseoir leur domination. Votre adversaire et vous-même êtes 2 de ces combattants, 2 Hérauts des Dieux.

C'est à la fois un jeu de micro-escarmouche et un jeu de carte, à 1 contre 1, où 2 joueurs s’affrontent au travers de leur figurine sur une arène (plateau de jeu A3) grâce à une combinaison astucieuse de cartes actions. Les cartes donnent ainsi vie à leur figurine.

Vous pourrez compter sur une faveur d’un Dieu au moment crucial du combat pour faire basculer la balance de votre côté mais méfiez-vous de leur courroux !

Chaque tour, vous choisissez des cartes Actions et en jouez une simultanément. Chaque carte peut se lire dans les 2 sens, à vous de faire le bon choix avant de les révéler. Ensuite, résolvez les effets. Les blessures provoqueront des réorientations et des reculs des figurines sur le plateau …

Chaque blessure vous fait perdre une carte de votre deck. Dès que vous perdez 9 cartes de votre deck, c’est la fin, votre fin !

boardgame: Naturally Disastrous: The Mother of All Expansions
description:
The dark secrets the Mad Scientist has kept hidden are now exposed in this new expansion to Naturally Disastrous.  This means your team can now explore the Vault and resolve new Encounters.  The Encounters you reveal will either help the natives or help you.

Contents:
4 vault tiles
3 mother ship tiles
5 new double sided alien cards
1 double sided jungle tile with jungle tokens
21 vault encounter tokens
3 space fighter markers
1 tank marker
1 guard marker

—description from the publisher

The Hitters Pack is a small, booster pack expansion to our wildly popular game, Bottom of the 9th. In the Hitters Pack players get 12 new batter cards to pick from and add to their all-star line up.
—description from the publisher

The Pitchers Pack is a small, booster pack expansion to our wildly popular game, Bottom of the 9th. In the Pitchers Pack players get 12 new pitcher cards to pick from and add to their all-star line up.
—description from the publisher

The border counties of Bhargive are unstable and dangerous. In the interests of commerce, neighboring lords send in soldiers to control the bandit problem. The trick is that the bandits are just troops sent from neighboring lords on the other side.

Players are barons with dreams of glory. Each turn, they roll dice in order to muster troops and send them to loot border counties - or to protect border counties. Whichever.

This game uses a Decktet and dice.

Pucks in Deep is a ice hockey themed card game for terrible players

Unlike many of the party games you’ve played before, Pucks in Deep is specifically designed to be as vulgar and disgusting as your beer league teammates.

The game is simple. Each round, one player asks a question from a Puck card and everyone else answers with their funniest Ice card.

Inside each Pucks in Deep box is 460 Ice cards and 90 Puck cards for a practically limitless amount of ice time. A referee card is included in each game box as well, and trust us, it's super satisfying to finally be able to put the ref in the box for a change.

—description from the publisher

Originally Given out to winners of selected events at Origins and GenCon of 2017. Said to later be a prize of Organize Play for stores.

This promo is designed to work with mechanics from DC Deck-Building Game: Crossover Pack 6 – Birds of Prey.

This Promo is a Hero Card, with Cost 2, and Worth 1 VP.

Card Text:

Ongoing: When this card rotates upright, draw a card and this card gains a card type of your choice this turn.

Each time you play a card that shares a card type with this card, rotate this card 90° Clockwise.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: This Belongs in a Museum: Talisman Promo Cards
description:
Set of 5 promo cards.

4 cards of players colors
1 instruction card.

Take the Talisman that matches your Archaeologist.  Place it in front of you with the unbroken Talisman side up.

Use the power of the Talisman to grant safe passage for your Archaeologist through an area controlled by any Mummy.

Once you have used the Talisman's ability, flip the card over to show that it's power is not longer available to your Archaeologist.

The Heroes of Karth Deathmatch Basic Game is available for FREE at PNP Arcade as a print-to-play version with the original color art!  Other decks will also be added throughout 2022!! Check it out at https://www.pnparcade.com/products/heroes-of-karth-deathmatch-basic-edition.

About the Deck Building Card Game:

Heroes of Karth: Deathmatch is a fantasy deck-building card game based on the Heroes of Karth book series. Choose 2 hero cards to lead your unit cards! Arm yourself with spell and treasure cards!! Then hire and position your cards onto the battlefield, with victory coming to the first to collect 40 unspent gold or 25 kill points!

Players try and outwit their opponent’s strategy in a tabletop game where the cards are the game pieces and players use gold to spawn additional cards onto the battlefield. Balance between using gold to hire units, mercenaries and heroes and saving your gold for the win condition. Utilize a wide range of strategies including ranged or melee classes, spells, pets, magic items, traps, poison, stealth units, healers, totems, and the undead curse. With over 200 cards rendered with stunningly beautiful original artwork, the deck-building choices are unlimited. Play a deathmatch where the careful management of resources and placement of your cards can make the difference between winning and losing!

The Story

From deep in the bowels of the Mines of Taas to the human kingdom of Hathaway, the Curse of the Undead is spreading across Green Valley like the vilest of plagues, wiping out all life in its path. Employing his army of shades to drain their life energy, Xeon the Undead Shadow Dragon has destroyed thousands of worlds throughout the millennia, and the world of Karth is next in his path of destruction. A chance meeting between Llythwain, autistic son of an elf Ranger and Killian, an undead shade has altered the familiar pattern of events, sowing the seeds of a monumental clash between the living and the dead, between good and evil, between the Allegiance and the Shard and Xeon's ferocious undead army. "The Undead Curse threatens Green Valley! The human necromancers are to blame!" With the utterance of those words by the Elven Lords, the Allegiance pact between humans, elves, dwarves, gnomes, and barbarians weakens, just as that of the Shard races grows in strength. By uniting the ogres, goblins, spiders, orcs, kobolds and hill giants under one banner, Xeon has awakened ancient hatred between the two mighty factions and the Shard is ready for war. A group of heroes, a human, gnome, and two dwarves led by an elf mage must gather the strength and courage to unlock the secret of the Undead Curse, but their time is running out.

The Charterstone Recharge Pack contains the components for a second campaign of Charterstone. It is completely optional as Charterstone is designed to be infinitely replayable after the first twelve-game legacy campaign.

The back side of the game board (included in the core game) is used for the second campaign. It contains all components that were permanently changed in the first campaign while allowing players to use unchanged components like wooden resources, meeples, and metal coins.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
In this hilarious party game, players are transported to Valhalla, were they play as both gods and mortals. In the feasting hall, the mortals try to impress the gods with their tales of battle, wits and folly!
 Each round, one player is a Norse god, and the rest of the players are mortals trying to get the god to choose their tale over the others. The tales are a combination of an Action card, the feat they performed, and a Challenge card, the obstacle they faced. Players guess the type of tale that god would want to hear most and lay the cards before them. The god will read the tales and choose one they wish to hear told aloud. That mortal wins the favor of the god and gets to tell their tale. The player who impresses the gods the most wins! What's your tale?

boardgame: Stratego Legends: Landor's Legion & Kralc's Horde Booster Pack
description:
Combo booster box released by Jumbo in 2001 that contained 7 gray and 7 white pieces. The sticker sheets in various boxes were different. The sticker in the lower right of the sticker sheet corresponded to the sticker in the upper right hand corner of the box so that buyers would know what they were getting.

Landor's Legion & Kralc's Horde were originally released separately in the US by Avalon Hill, with 15 pieces in the box.

Take on the role of a fearless mech warrior, and defend Earth from invasion!

Build your mech, gain ‘Battle dice’, and inflict massive damage to the enemy. Every successful engagement will earn you Battle Points (BPS), skill cards and resources, allowing you to upgrade your warrior and mech.

But beware, many enemies will be too powerful to take on alone so you’ll need to work with others to defend the planet. But how much and with who is your choice, as a problem shared means half the reward and, more importantly, half the fame. After all, you can’t put the world’s finest mech warriors in the same room and not expect some ‘friendly’ rivalry!

Don’t be too greedy though, if the threat level climbs too high, Earth will be overrun and everyone loses!

Description from the publisher:

The Europa Incident is a science fiction boardgame about the control of the most strategically and economically useful sites in the Main Asteroid Belt of our Solar System by mega-corporations, transnational agencies, and armed organizations. The game uses a partially random hand of card with deckbuilding mechanic through which it is possible to use available resources for infrastructure construction, technological development and conflict management. The Europa Incident mixes typically “managerial” gaming elements to strategic expansion and conquest dynamics enhanced by a chit-pull combat resolution system. Effective expansion of control zones is the key and it must be fast. Fighting, on the other side, can be useful, but it will be the one who first consolidate its own expansion to be more likely to win.
Set 1 features 4 factions: Anglo-Atlantic Alliance, Central Scientific Committee, NeoViridians and ZhangSpace

Description from the publisher:

AOX is a traditional-style abstract strategy game for 2 players.

In AOX, players take turns moving tiles in an attempt to defeat their opponent in one of two ways:

1. Capture 3 of the opponents tiles
OR,
2. Move a triangle tile into a highlighted space on the opponents board edge

However, neither of these conditions are easy to achieve, as each turn a player may only move specific tiles as designated by the tokens drawn from their bag. In addition to this, the active player each turn will have the option to strategically 'swap' a token with the opposing player, changing the number of each type of token in both players bags, thus shifting the odds of being able to move certain tiles. AOX is a game of strategy, patience and balance.

Made entirely from laser-cut wood, AOX has a traditional and classy appearance. The gameplay and concept of AOX is very simple, though the level of strategic depth is great.
These Bonus Tiles are used as a alternatives of the regular Permanent Ability Tiles.

Höflinge (Aktion - Kosten 5 [Münzen])

    
    
        Decke eine Handkarte auf.
Für jeden Kartentyp (Aktion, Angriff ...),
den sie hat, wähle eine andere Option:
+1 Aktion
+1 Kauf
+3 [Münzen] oder
nimm ein Gold vom Vorrat.

    


---

Original text:




    
        Courtier (Action - Cost 5 [coin])

    
    
        Reveal a card from your hand. For each type it has (Action, Attack, etc.), choose one:
+1 Action, or +1 Buy, or +3 [coins], or gain a Gold from the supply.

    


---

This card is also part of Dominion: Intrigue – Update Pack.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Welcome everyone to humiliated the game where you will laugh, cry, and be humiliated. The first person draws a duel card and go against someone and they play(rock paper scissors, flex off to a macho song or head bang to a rock song.) The winner gets a point and the loser has to draw a humiliated card which is where the real fun starts. Having to pick a humiliated could be something that is not that bad or it could be something awful (someone throws a hot dog at your face, draw a 70s porno mustache on your face with a marker, or the bukake party) Win the most duels and you're the winner . The stories you will have after you play will be unforgettable. Grab this game, get some laughs, and tell your friends.

—description from the publisher

Description from the publisher:

Add even more tactical options and units to your Hoplomachus games with the city of Machu Picchu! Featuring an all new Champion (Walla Cuna) and units to bring it up to date with every previous game and expansion, Carthage offers units with brand new Skills, Alt Attacks, and Innate Abilities!

Description from the publisher:

Add even more tactical options and units to your Hoplomachus games with the city of Carthage! Featuring an all new Champion (Dido) and units to bring it up to date with every previous game and expansion, Carthage offers units with brand new Skills, Alt Attacks, and Innate Abilities!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Mage Wars: Academy – Warlord Expansion includes a new mage for use in both Mage Wars: Academy and Mage Wars: Arena. In this expansion set, play as a relentless Warlord competing to prove that your army is the most powerful weapon!

A cooperative board game for 1-4 super science revolutionaries.
As youthful super scientist revolutionaries you wield a limited supply of near magical technology cards
and dice.

Your goal is to overthrow the fascist government and rescue the city before your resources are depleted and progress is destroyed.

Lucid City was a paradise, the home of innovative and artistic superscience. Then came the Undoing and life was nearly extinguished by greedy bureaucratic zealots. They suppressed progress, released engineered plagues and destroyed most valuable tech to consolidate power. All hope was lost.

Skillful and efficient play, converting limited innovation cards and renewable dice technology, is required to save the day.
But with each step towards enlightenment, the enemy will try to counter your progress towards illumination. Tense, exciting play forces the players to integrate their actions, and use their resources with great wisdom.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Ninja: The card game of sneak attacks and captures
description:
Description from the publisher:

Ninja is fast paced, fun, and engaging. 2-5 players compete simultaneously to be the top ninja. Choose up to 3 cards from your hand and attack the central Castle. If you have the highest attack, you capture the Castle.  Look out for the Castle Guard who blocks attacks. Use the Black Ninja to double your attack. Be the first ninja to 11 Castle points and win!

The name Quattele is the short form of "Quattro Elementa" - 4 elements, because in this strategy game for 2 - 4 players fire, earth, water and air fight against each other alone or in 2 teams.

The special feature of Quattele is, that each element can only beat one of the other elements and is beaten itself by one. So, for example, water beats fire, but is beaten by earth. Water and air meet as allies.

The rules are straightforward and the moves are basically simple, which is why players can quickly find themselves in the game. However, due to its special set of rules, Quattele offers a high degree of tactical challenge. The players have to attack as well as try to defend the opposing, allied element from the attacks of the opponents.

If you manage to reduce the 10 pieces of the opponent's elements by hitting them to 3 or to occupy the middle of the opponent's starting field, you win the battle of the elements.

—description from the publisher

Odin, the Allfather, ruler of Asgard, and protector of the Nine Realms, has gone missing. Now, the world tree Yggdrasil is in turmoil. The gods of Asgard fight amongst themselves, vying for the right to rule and to avert the catastrophe.

Runes of Ragnarok is a dice-based elimination game for 2-4 players. It features over 40 custom dice, 4 heroes, and 24 creatures! Take on the role of one of Asgard’s legendary gods, and battle against other players for control of the Nine Realms. Cast the runes, summon mighty creatures, and wield powerful magic as you battle your foes in this free for all game of wits and strategy.

Objective: Be the last Hero standing.

Heroes: Play as one of 4 Heroes in RoR: Thor, Freya, Heimdall, or Sif. Each has a different set of powers and must be ranked up through play to access all of their abilities.

Runes: Roll the Rune Dice each turn. Various combinations of the runes will grant you Focus (the game currency) or any of 12 different spells (in 4 categories). Spells can be used in combat to strengthen your army or attack your Heroes.

Minions: RoR features 24 different creatures (8 types with 3 variants each) which each have their own set of custom dice and combat abilities. Purchase minions with Focus to add them to your Battle Unit. Attack your enemies and prove that you have the strength and might to rule Asgard in Odin's absence!

—description from the publisher

Spirit Realms: Tengoku-SR is a 2-4 player army fort builder card game that revolves around a retelling of the gods as animal warrior spirits called Spirit Masters. Each warrior is Ruler of 1 of the 9 realms in the world of Tengoku, SR Prime being the first realm. Each realm is represented by a Spirit Master who comes with his own deck of power, each deck with it's own strengths and weakness. Some decks are magic based, with tech elements some are Mental based with Earth elements while some decks are defensive and others are passive. You can choose which characters and deck(s) represent your goals and personal strategy, and play them as stand alones or combine them with other decks to create your own unique decks.

Players must protect their throne by defending their forts from being destroyed while attempting to capture their opponent's Throne. Players get to build forts and barriers while using minions to build armies, while learning basic gameplay players are able to invent unique strategies.

"A New Twist on Dominoes!"

Description from the back of box (with edits):

Match colors instead of dots in this mind-spinning pattern matching game. But the swirling tiles aren't the only things to make you dizzy. Another twist? EARN POINTS for the tiles you play next to, not for the tile you play. The more high-point tiles you can match in (a) single turn, the more you score. Spiroz is easy to learn, but you'll soon discover that a well-planned strategy wins the game.

Originally released in 2009 as Dizios by Mindware.

Sbires: Le Sanctuaire is an expansion for the Sbires base game that consists of 26 new cards for nine new effects that might assist you in becoming the next Senechal of Montfleury! Will you meet the Sauvageonne to lose points of prestige to your opponents? Will you use the Bourrel to discard cards to your opponents?

This game consists of two sub-games. In the first game players take the role of journalists and try to get to the sat-van as quick as possible in order to upload the three pictures they have to capture in this game. The pictures are all Bavaria-buildings or sites and have a UFO sighting on them.

This is a Malefiz kind of adaptation.

In the second part the game, journalists try to get the cover-story before the other players can by running up the stairs of the publisher trying to beat the others to his office.

Description from the publisher:

Bobata the bell ringer's awesome weapon is at the disposal of the Savage Wave. As Bobata swings the mighty bell around his head he is equally inspiring to allies as he is fear inducing to his foes.

CW-M10 was available during the Cthulhu Wars Onslaught Two Kickstarter campaign. It includes the 6-8 player versions of the Earth map (CW-M4), Primeval map (CW-M6), Dreamlands map (CW-M7), Yuggoth map (CW-M8), and Library at Celaeno map (CW-M9). A plain white thin-walled box holds the maps together.

Description from the publisher:

Only the shrewdest and cruellest Sho-Bakemono survive to become a Hexer. Wu-Zang manipulates the fates, cursing opponents before disappearing into the shadows.

Neptune and the fish is played on a playing field 5x5.
Both players have different goals. Black (Neptune) plays with 3 stones and tries to hit either all the white stones, or position himself so that he cannot hit another stone.
A white stone, which is horizontally or vertically adjacent, is hit.

White (the fish) plays with 23 stones and tries to get black in a position where all 3 black stones are in the same row or column. White moves a stone to a free neighboring field, white is not able do remove black stones.

A very original unsymmetrical and pretty fast paced tactical game for 2 persons.

Description from the publisher:

Waka finds happiness in the smaller things in life. In fact nothing delights him more than lobbing his favourite toy, a huge stone Buddha head, at anyone unlucky enough to get in the way of his rampages. Those that attempt to get in close, find him just as satisfied to bring it violently, crashing down on their heads! Many foes’ last moments have been filled with the bass rumble of Waka’s chortling.

Description from the publisher:

The mischievous, lazy pair have tied their war drum to the back of a slave. They ride into battle charged with getting the best out of their companions, the pounding rhythm of the drum drives the Bakemono on to achieve their full potential.

Description from the publisher:

The Slaver is an abomination even by the standards of his Kin. His worth to the Savage Wave is unquestionable and the constant warring for control between the twin heads; Kemono and Ushi can leave opponents struggling for a strategy to deal with the brute. Kemono’s primal desire to smash his way through an enemy force is tempered by the more considered and manipulative techniques of Ushi.
 Blister contains one miniature, one 50mm base and three full colour profile cards.

Description from the publisher:

Tra-peng has distinguished himself from the horde of his brothers. Stronger, faster, more skilled, such attributes in a Bakemono are rare due to the jealousy invoked and the inevitable dagger found in one’s back. However the support Tra-peng receives from his bat companion combined with his own physical dominance has allowed him to attain an almost revered state amongst his peers.

Description from the publisher:

The legends speak of ferocious beasts 
terrorizing small hamlets in the dark of night, these stories are now told with added gravitas in the remote and isolated villages of Jwar. The return of the Nian is bad enough but the affinity between them and the Oni stalking the land will inevitably be a severe blow to humanity in the dark days ahead.

Description from the publisher:

It is unknown when the beasts of the wave started taking captives. These are no hostages though, they are put to work or put to the sword by their less than benign captors. The blood of the Oni sends the slaves into a rage on the battlefield, allowing them to achieve feats of strength they would not have dreamed of back in the fields.

Description from the publisher:

Any who oppose the Savage Wave quickly come to realise just how terrible and fearsome an enemy an Oni makes. It turns out their youth are just as fierce, standing as tall as a fully grown man they take up the fight of the Savage Wave readily and with a lust for battle only possible from a member of this demonic race.

Description from the publisher:

The first of the Dai Bakemono to come to the fore, bigger and stronger than all but the Rinsho Bakemono, Uk-Kang has earned renowned for being fiercely protective of his brethren and it has been noted by the Council of Shadows.

Description from the publisher:

Another young Oni joins the ranks of the Savage Wave. Boba is a wicked shot, picking enemies off at will. When he channels his Ki he can lob his heavy iron shots with disconcerting accuracy. Those that have felt themselves safely out of his range are only proved wrong once.

Description from the publisher:

Those who taste the blood of a demon are driven mad by the raw alien power coursing through their veins, warping their senses and souls. Jun was already mad. An evil and impure man the Oni’s mad dog to set upon their enemies, savage and loyal to his masters he will do anything for the next taste, putting himself in harm’s way without a thought for his own safety.

Description from the publisher:

Deep in the belly of the world in the dark places the Bakemono tribes call their own the bats grow large. Whether it is through the consumption of the rare and toxic fungus that the bats in the warrens grow to such size or because the Sho Bak Shugenja are not opposed to the occasional Bakemono snack to help with the diet no one could comment. Those brave explorers who have ventured deep enough into caverns where the bats dwell don’t live long after hearing a strange clicking. As in battle the bats sweep in from the skies, driving their targets to the ground, their suffering only ended then the bat has had its fill of their blood or when the noxious toxics the bats carry in the mouths have overwhelmed their bodies.

Description from the publisher:

For so long have the Shugenja of the Bakemono tribes lived and worked with darkness and shadow that they seem to see it almost as a living breathing entity. They whisper, listen and learn from the deep blackness in which they dwell. Where others see emptiness they see power and an ancient knowledge. So long have they lingered there that in battle the Shugenja are able to harness the endless dark strength of the shadows to grip enemies in place to be hacked to pieces, or to shroud ones very soul obscuring it from the light. Those who have lived to tell the tale are never the same and many seem lost after, the light behind their eyes extinguished forever.

Tombed is a dungeon-style game from 2 to 5 players set in Egypt in the 1940s with a fantasy hue. In this game, a group of adventurers enters an ancient Egyptian temple to fulfill a contract that will make them rich and famous. What the brave adventurers ignore is that their presence will awaken ancient pharaohs...

One of the players controls one of the possible Pharaohs (each with different abilities and powers), while the others each play as one of the adventurers. The Pharaoh player tries to gain enough power to return to life, and the adventurers try to accomplish their contracts and to leave the Temple alive.

boardgame: Dirk Gently's Holistic Detective Agency: Everything is Connected
description:
Dirk Gently's Holistic Detective Agency: Everything is Connected is the first in a series of "Everything is Connected" storytelling games in which the mysteries are only as looney as the players.

In this game, a detective and a holistic detective put together the clues, accuse a person of interest, and tell their assistants the story of the crime. The assistants then process the two versions of the case and simultaneously select which version of the truth is more believable. To solve the case, you have to think on your feet and remember that "everything is connected".

A card game version of Kimble (aka Trouble). Promotional product for Reilu -bread brand. Comes with 55 cards and rules.

Players compete to have two of their color pawn -cards make a round around four slots in the table, one slot in front of every player. Pawncards move by playing movecards, and usually they make other players pawncards move also. If other player's pawncard is in front of you, you can play a card that "eats" the pawn, sending it back to it owners hand.

boardgame: Police Precinct: Michelle Schroeder Character Board
description:
Was available through Police Precinct - Online! kickstarter campaign as well as being available at Dice Tower Con 2016

Text:
Unorthodox: At any time before taking your action, you may discard any one of your police cards to temporarily increase your strength in any area by 1 (e.g. investigation goes from 2 up to 3)
Underdog: As a community service officer, you get more donuts for arresting than others (one donut for 1 or 2 punk tokens and two donuts for 3 or more punks)

Description from the publisher:

Of the many types of Bakemono the Dai-Bakemono make the best shock troopers. Despite their low intelligence which can on occasion force their leaders to change their plans quickly they provide the Bakemono tribes with resilient, hardy and aggressive troops. When their fury is stoked they emit fearsome roars that echo around there deep subterranean caverns and strike fear into the hearts of any who are the subject of their aggression, rushing wildly to smash the foes of the tribes into the dirt. Of the many types of Bakemono the Dai-Bakemono make the best shock troopers. Despite their low intelligence which can on occasion force their leaders to change their plans quickly they provide the Bakemono tribes with resilient, hardy and aggressive troops. When their fury is stoked they emit fearsome roars that echo around there deep subterranean caverns and strike fear into the hearts of any who are the subject of their aggression, rushing wildly to smash the foes of the tribes into the dirt.

Blister contains one miniature, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

Description from the publisher:

A terrible and furious oni, Yusha the vindictive is a master of controlling, focusing and leashing the destructive ferocity of those unlucky enough to find themselves under his lash. Whether it’s the vicious lion like Kaihei, blood maddened slaves or a gaggle of Bakemono eager to prove themselves to this hulking master. Yusha’s command of the Kaihei comes from his position amongst them as the undisputed pride alpha, his own feral nature allowing him to easily assume dominance over a pride and drive them to almost breaking point, through rage and frequent strings of the lash.

Blister contains one model, one 50mm base and one full colour profile card.

Description from the publisher:

Eight foot of lean muscle, a cunning, ruthless hunter. The Kaihei Alpha are the large and most ferocious of the Kaihei an already deadly beast. Like all packs it only functions with strong leadership and Alphas have this in spades, another creature born out of the unforgiving wilderness the Oni call home where only the strongest survive. In battle, under the Kaihei Alphas glare the pack moves as one, quickly and decidedly devouring their helpless prey alive, snapping and ripping in a bloody fury of tooth and claw. Of the many fighting beasts of the Oni the Kaihei rightly hold a place of feared in the minds of men.

Description from the publisher:

These muscled hunters are pack animals and whilst fearsome in isolation they are truly terrifying when running in a group. Lean and agile they bound across the battlefield, throwing themselves with great momentum at their quarry, barrelling them to the ground whilst another in the pack snaps and tears at the exposed vital areas of the prey. Teeth and claws as hard as steel they can tear through even the toughest of Minimoto iron with a concentration bite.

Description from the publisher:

A monster of huge proportions even by Oni standards. On the battlefield he rampages through enemy lines, neither man nor metal can halt his carnage as he scatters soldiers like ants under his colossal shadow, swinging his desecrated tori arch in wide arcs. Those not broken by his initial impact are drilled mercilessly into the dirt as easily as a carpenter hammering nails, even the strongest armour offering little protection against his unworldly strength.

Description from the publisher:

Rinsho are prized within Bakemono society for their enormous strength matched only by their stupidity, making them perfect shock troops. These gangly Bakemono are blessed with vastly accelerated regenerative powers, able to regrow limbs and heal what would be mortal wounds as if minor graze. Their durability offers some staying power to the Bakemono battle lines, anchoring key points on the battle field, weathering the brunt of the enemy offense and enabling a swarming counter punch. Bringing down these giant Bakemono is no mean feat as injury only sends them spiralling into a furious whirlwind and violence.

Description from the publisher:

A thousand pair of red eyes glowing in the darkness, a thousand fangs thirsting for blood. The Wave and the Council of Shadow’s Shugenja have many tricks ready to play in the game of war. Now they have unleashed nightwings from their cage of rock and blackness. Like leather winged locusts they tear through the countryside, gorging on those unfortunate enough to be caught in their path, leaving only the withered husks, sucked dry as a reminder of their unquenchable thirst.

Description from the publisher:

A lot could be said for the subterranean races’ savage and cruel practices however their ability to adapt and change based on the environment and resources available cannot be ignored, their resourcefulness is something to be admired when considering the scarcity of materials offered in the dark places they claim as their own. Now with the world in turmoil, ravaged by war, the bakemono tribes pick the battlefields clean like maggots at a wound. This has led to the advancements of the Prefecture being turned against them. The boomers are one such example of this, two brave and possibly a little stupid bakemono eagerly working the famous ryu arquebus to deadly effect.

Description from the publisher:

As the war fought above and below ground intensifies all sides are pushed to find more weapons and soldiers to turn the tide in their favour. The Council of Shadows is no different and selects the bravest and foolhardiest of the bakemono warriors to capture and master the beasts that live in the shadows. Only the few survive, but those that do are able to ride these murderous monsters into battle with devastating effect.

Please be advised that this model requires Giant Cave Bat (BSW015) or The Nian (BSW008) to be played in game but can be used in conjunction with either.

Description from the publisher:

It is hard to tell if Xi-Han had lost his mind before or after he consumed every unusual mushroom he could find. The exact purpose of this pursuit remains a mystery. What is clear is that bizarrely he has managed to develop the ability to focus and force his body to replicate the effects of these rare and potent fungi. Sending his body into spasm and unleashing the deranged and violent “Zoo” for a short while to wreak havoc on his enemies. He also still maintains a constant supply of mushrooms on his person that he can throw in battle to Blind, Frighten or enrage anyone unlucky enough to inhale the spores.

Description from the publisher:

An Onisho forged in battle, beaten and bent over years of combat into a bundle of muscle and murderous energy. Years have taught Goro to anticipate the moves of his opponents, enabling him to time his offense with deadly precision. Knowing exactly when to use multiple crippling strikes or a single brutal blow to finish.

boardgame: Bushido: Savage Wave Special Card Pack 1
description:
Description from the publisher:

Special cards are an interesting and flexible way to create very a focussed, individual and distinct Warband for your favourite faction. There are three types of Special Cards and each work slightly differently but each in their own way enables will enable you to customise your Warbands to fit the play style or theme you want. These packs contain an assortment of Enhancement, Event and Terrain Special cards that range from martial techniques to ancient shrines, acts of amazing heroism to soul draining terror. Each pack contains thematically fitting cards to adhere to the factions strengths, weaknesses and in game feel.

Pack contains fourteen Special cards: Seven faction specific and seven generic cards.

boardgame: Bushido: Savage Wave Special Card Pack 2
description:
Description from the publisher:

Special cards are an interesting and flexible way to create a very focussed, individual and distinct Warband for your favourite faction. There are four types of Special Cards and each work slightly differently but each in their own way will enable you to customise your Warbands to fit the play style or theme you want. These packs contain an assortment of Enhancement, Event, Terrain and Theme Special cards that range from martial techniques to ancient shrines, acts of amazing heroism to soul draining terror. Each pack contains thematically fitting cards to adhere to the faction’s strengths, weaknesses and in game feel.

Pack contains fourteen faction specific special cards

boardgame: Dream Home: 156 Sunny Street – Game Room Promo Card
description: Single promo card to 156 Sunny Street expansion. It is additional "friend" card which gives you 3 points for having Game Room adjacent to Bathroom or Kitchen in your home.

Carcassonne: Die Märkte zu Leipzig (German for "The markets of Leipzig"), is a small expansion for Carcassonne. It contains four double-landscape tiles depicting marketplaces. It is only available in the "Carcassonne II" design.

The expansion was first released as a limited edition of total 600 examples: the 1st 300 examples at the "modell hobby spiel" in Leipzig from the 29th of September to the 1st of October 2017, and the 2nd 300 examples at the Spiel'17 in Essen from 26th to 29th of October 2017. This limited version was signed by Klaus-Jürgen Wrede and the CEO of Leipzig's trade fair center (Messe Leipzig) and contain 13 Followers/Meeples. A more general version is also available.

Rules of the expansion affect the many small farmhouses shown on tiles of all "Carcassonne II" expansions.

It also contains two double-landscape tiles for Carcassonne: Amazonas.

Part of the Carcassonne series.

Catch-Up Game is a quiz game by Chris Budde, published by McClean Enterprises. The goal of the game is to answer questions from cards. Which question to read is determined by a die roll. The die have 6 sides: Easy, Hard, All, Choose, Easy and Hard. On a card there is two questions, one easy and one hard. If All is rolled, all players most answer one of the questions, the player selects (easy or hard). If Choose is rolled the player that rolled the die, must choose another player to answer one of the questions. The questions is about personal issues: What is your favorite color? or Name a personal goal.

Company of Iron is a squad-based skirmish game that reinvents WARMACHINE and HORDES battles as smaller confrontations between fewer warriors and their character leaders. Average games are played with 10–15 miniatures, and Company of Iron is compatible with the majority of the existing miniatures in the WARMACHINE and HORDES product lines. The rules for Company of Iron have been streamlined and do not include warcasters or warlocks but can include light warjacks and light warbeasts.

The Company of Iron box set is designed for two players and will contain two 20-point armies, including a unit of Cygnar Stormblades and a unit of Minion Farrow Brigands. Additionally, two all-new character solos will be included to lead their respective units: Lieutenant Gwen Keller and Agata, Queen of Carnage. These new models can be used in both Company of Iron and in WARMACHINE and HORDES. All components needed to play the game are in the box set, including rules, tokens, and two types of cards that allow players to choose specialties for their squad leaders as well as battle plans that can provide powerful effects and advantages in combat.

Company of Iron uses the core rules of WARMACHINE and HORDES but has been tailored for the scale of the game and the absence of warcasters and warlocks. Emphasizing its fast-paced skirmish scale and the focus on small squads and solos, gameplay revolves around players taking turns activating one model at a time as opposed to the alternating activation in WARMACHINE and HORDES, in which players activate their entire army during a single turn.

“We have designed Company of Iron as an ideal entry point for new players who want to play in the world of WARMACHINE and HORDES,” said Chief Creative Officer Matt Wilson. “But it’s also a product the existing WARMACHINE and HORDES community has been requesting for years, and it scratches that itch for players craving a fast-paced tactical experience that can be played with a shorter time commitment.”

—description from the publisher

Jump In is a fast-paced card game created by Lycee Classique Echternach students (Luxemburg) and developed by D. Kirps and C. Kruchten. The clever combination of dice and cards throws demands constant attention, good reflexes and provides high playing pleasure.
—description from the publisher (English)

Jump In ist ein reaktionsschnelles Kartenspiel, erfunden von Studenten des Lycee Classique Echternach (Luxemburg) und entwickelt durch D. Kirps und C. Kruchten. Die pfiffige Kombination aus wuerfeln und Karten abwerfen verbindet ständige Aufmerksamkeit, gute Reflexe und hohen Spielspass.
—description from the publisher (German)

Jump In est un jeu de cartes rapide invente par des etudiants du Lycee Classique echternach (Luxembourg) et developpe par D. Kirps et C. Kruchten. La combinaison intelligente de lancer le de et defausser ses cartes attire l'attention et necessite de bons reflexes chez les joueurs qui y prennent plaisir rapidement.
—description from the publisher (French)

Description from the publisher:

Fake News or Not? is a game about phony funny stories and ludicrous true tales!  Life writes the craziest stories, but what can we believe and what is just a pack of lies?

One player (the News Anchor for that round) reads a News Card to the other players.  Each News Card contains the first part of a news story and 4 possible conclusions to the story.  One conclusion is correct and the true (Real News) answer, but the others are made-up (Fake News) falsehoods.  The News Anchor reads the Real News answer and picks one Fake News answer to read.  The other players then vote on which they believe to be the Real News.  Each player who votes for the Real News answer receives 1 point.  A different player takes on the role of the News Anchor each round.  The game ends after each player has had two turns as the News Anchor and the player with the most points wins!

Honour is everything! Tribal provides players with a game set in a pre-gunpowder tribal/clan setting. The rules  offer some new and exciting approaches to wargaming.

In the book you get: the basic rules; optional rules for missile fire, dirty tricks and honour pools; four scenarios you can play in a variety of tribal settings; in-depth explanation of culture, weapons and combat for Maori, Vikings, Aztecs & Gladiators; extra rules for Stone-Age, Heian Japanese & Iroquois.

Honour is used to drive the narrative of the game. Players are rewarded in honour for the things that they do in the battle. Honour determines who wins Tribal rather than the normal determinants of wargames (killing enemy/taking ground). In turn, honour is a dynamic part of the game - it can be won and lost during combat, the deeds of combatants can win or lose honour, players can spend honour to do 'dirty tricks' to win combats etc.

The game is played with a normal deck of cards. Cards determine movement, unit activation and - most importantly - combat. While cards still have the randomness of the draw from the deck, players can actually be involved in the combat through playing/holding particular cards. Attacking, defending, waiting for an opening, bluffing the opponent - these are all strategies the player can use in a round of Tribal combat.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play

boardgame: Killer Bunnies and the Quest for the Magic Carrot: Fantastic Booster
description:
Description from the publisher:

Killer Bunnies are back, do a double take, with fan-inspired cards that will make you quake! The Fantastic Booster Deck adds 55 cards to your existing set plus 27 unique small cards!

New cards include: a Dated Dolla to make you holler, a Soft Kitty Warm Kitty to cure an ill and make you sound witty.  Staring contests are plenty of fun when you play the card Blinky Madison.  Use your phone to play Text Time or Trip's Trips for a new paradigm.  There's a nice nod to Apple's Steve and a Drazi Feud if you can believe!

Bunny Triplets are for the birds, dispersed in part by Angry Nerds, and if you can count to 99 then you'll find Carrot N quite fine.  Full steam ahead, we're back on track, Fantastic is a new booster you should not lack!

boardgame: Killer Bunnies and the Quest for the Magic Carrot: Caramel Swirl Booster
description:
Description from the publisher:

Drenched in sweetness, new and delicious, the next Killer Bunnies Booster Deck is witty and auspicious! Caramel Swirl adds 55 cards to your existing set plus a brown 12-sided die.

Other alluring cards include the feminine forces of the Verminator or Omega Mu, and Johnny Wishbone to test psychic abilities for you.  Funky Fungus affects a weapon card and the Sacred Chalice gives you a financial vanguard.  Cherry Cordial will keep the game clean but Alternate Uhura will prove quite mean.

The most powerful bunnies will take note when you play the card Island Vote.  You might say "Fuff" and you might say "Ew" when you're asked to play Smell That Shoe!

Do yourself a favor and treat yourself to an all-new flavor.  Take the new deck for a whirl, the delicate and delectable Caramel Swirl!

Age of Kingdoms (AOK) is an epic-fantasy 4x board game where players lead the nations of Laruna in their quest to earn the favor of the gods. As powerful magic wielding rulers, players will fight, trade, and spell-sling their way into the god’s good graces in their race to earn the most glory before the end of time. 
With thought provoking design, this worker placement-miniature battle hybrid will have players utilizing strategy to defeat one another through careful use of spells, territory control, unit advancement and economic development.
 Choosing between 10 ruler archetypes, 6 kingdoms and over 40 recruitable units, players will earn glory by way of war, trade, or mutual achievements, allowing them versatility in both their tactics and playstyle.

—description from the publisher

Part magical university, part museum, part tourist attraction, the great Collegium of Artistry had flourished in Felstad's final days. Under the leadership of the seemingly immortal Malcor the Mad, the vast complex expanded, with new wings being built wherever they would fit, including up and down the rock face, and even buried within the mountain itself. Visitors called it one of the architectural wonders of the world; the students, who often got lost in its endless tunnels, simply called it "The Maze."

With a titanic crash, an immense ice shelf tears free from the mountains that that loom above Frostgrave, revealing the lost Collegium, and the race for its secrets begins. The Maze is known to have contained many rare and unique treasures, and who knows what may have survived . . .

This new, bumper-sized supplement for Frostgrave contains a host of new adventures, treasures, and creatures to challenge players. It also includes its own, unique campaign and experience system, as well as information about several of the mythical lost schools of magic.

—description from the publisher

Long ago, the great Colossus of Argantheon stood upon one of the outer islands, its vast, outstretched arm pointing the way to the Crystal Pool, or so the ancient texts claim. Then, during some forgotten war, the Colossus was shattered, and its parts flung across the Archipelago, to lie in forgotten ruin.

In this expansion for Frostgrave: Ghost Archipelago, players lead their warbands through a series of interlinked scenarios in a race to discover the secrets of the Colossus. Along the way, they will encounter new monsters, discover new treasures, and even recruit a few new specialist warriors to their cause. Perhaps, if they are successful, the knowledge they obtain will help point them towards the ultimate treasure!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Runewars Miniatures Game: Uthuk Y'llan Army Expansion
description:
A new evil force enters the battle for Terrinoth with the Uthuk Y'llan Army Expansion for Runewars Miniatures Game. The army of the Locust Horde includes four new demonic units with the Uthuk Berserks, Flesh Rippers, Spine Threshers, and their dark hero Ravos the Everhungry. In addition to twenty-two customizable figurines, the expansion includes tokens, terrain pieces and cards, and twelve upgrade cards.

—description from the publisher

Riga: 5+6 Player Expansion does just what you might expect — allow for games of Riga to include up to six players. To do this, this expansion includes 55 cards, including 22 ware cards, 24 building cards, and 8 Riga-specific building cards.

boardgame: DC Comics Dice Masters: Terry McGinnis Promo Cards
description:
Promo cards created for the Rainbow Draft Weekend 2, including full art card, sketch variant (blank) card and die.

Terry McGinnis: Batman Beyond card text:
When you purchase a non-Basic Action Die with printed cost less than the number of your active characters, add it to your Prep Area.
Max: 4

boardgame: Marvel Dice Masters: Melinda May Promo Cards
description:
Promo cards created for the Rainbow Draft Weekend 2, including full art card, sketch variant (blank) card and die.

Melinda May: Agent of S.H.I.E.L.D. card text:
While you have another active character die, Melinda May has Iron Will. 
Teamwatch - Deal 1 damage to target character die or opponent. 
MAX: 4

OOF-DAH! is an American Specialty tabletop boardgame for 2-4 players.  It has educational content, but the real focus is on fun, competition, and endlessly creative and varied gameplay.  The theme is based on organic farming, and the goal is to collect Hunger Tokens by growing organic vegetables on your farm, and feeding local communities. If you play strategically and negotiate successfully with fellow players, or Growers, you can increase your odds of doing well at the Farmer's Market, which dictates crop values and market pricing.  
     Game-play consists of turn-based die rolls, accompanied by trading between turns.  Multiple events may occur on die rolls, allowing for a nonlinear and more engaging style of play.  Players, or Growers, must adapt on the fly to ever-changing in-game conditions.  A Grower may only score an OOF-DAH! if they grow the right crops on the right cover crops, and if they harvest the correct crops according to the ideal growing season.
     Additional scoring opportunities are facilitated by novel features such as Crop Combos, Crop Cartels, Undercutting, and targeted Guard Token protections.  Only the savviest Growers with the keenest sense of what's hot and what's not in the Market, will meet all challenges, earning the right to say, "OOF-DAH!"

The earths most loathsome villains are at it again. Causing destruction and widespread panic on a level never before seen. As you attempt to make contact with the rest of the team, the WATCHTOWER is attacked.

Seconds before communications went dark, a partial message was intercepted from the plant APOKALIPS. The sourct of the message can mean only one thing - DARKSEID.
The details are vague, but the message was clear; DARKSEID calls for the destruction of the JLA. Cut off from the rest of the team, you rush out in search of the JLA's most nefarious villains in order to stop DARKSEID's evil plot.

Travel through the game board collecting information on DARKSEID's master plan. Carry out board actions, collect HIT TOKENS, and acquire TOLL PASSES while you dish out hard justice in HENCHMEN BATTLES. Gain access to the criminal underworld that lead to ENEMY STRONGHOLDS in your search of Earth's most loathsome villains. Return the villains to your BASE CAMP for information to help you foil DARKSEID's sinister plot. The first person to complete their SCORE CARD by Acquiring 4 unique VILLAIN TOKENS and return them to their BASE CAMP is the winner.
—description from the publisher

boardgame: Apocrypha Adventure Card Game: Mr. Whiffle Promo Card
description:
Apocrypha Adventure Card Game by Lone Shark Games can be played as a cooperative card game or a role-playing game set in a pre-apocalyptic world.  Their own Patrick Rothfuss contributed to the storytelling. Impress your friends with this horribly awesome Mr Whiffle card!

All proceeds go to Worldbuilders.

Body Gift
Discard: Boost Body.
Support - Slot: While Slotted,
-active saint Donate to Charity: Draw 1 new Omen.  If it is an Omen of Doom, sacrifice Mr. Whiffle.

Keywords: Dreamer, Ally, Tool, Charm

boardgame: Sentinels of the Multiverse: Oversized Villain Cards
description:
These 4" by 6" cards represent all of the villains from the Sentinels of the Multiverse core game as well as the Rook City, Infernal Relics, Shattered Timelines, and Wrath of the Cosmos expansions. This set of 31 oversized villain cards even comes with villain character cards for each of the villain mini-expansions and promos, including two new promos never seen before!

Sentinels of the Multiverse: Villain Oversized Cards was previously released as an accessory.

Publisher Description

Star Commandos is an adventure game--played with miniature figures--which simulates futuristic combat during the great Rebellion against the interstellar Galactan Empire. Here is a realistic game in which you choose the troops and weapon-types you wish to use, and then control the action in a daring commando raid in the far future.

User Summary

A Galacta series game from Heritage USA's Paint 'N' Play line.

The Paint 'N' Play games all featured Heritage miniatures along with paints and painting instructions. Unlike the other Paint 'N' Play games, which featured a board, the Galacta series games were straight miniatures rules designed to be played directly on the tabletop.

The contents included metal miniature figures, paints, a brush, "adventure game rules", and a 12-sided die. Heritage sold additional Galacta 25mm figures that could be used with the games.

Publisher Description

Galactic Rebellion is an adventure game--played with miniature figures--which simulates futuristic combat during the great Rebellion against the interstellar Galactan Empire. Here is a realistic game in which you choose the troops and weapons you wish to use, and then command the attack (or defense).

Included in Galactic Rebellion are stormtroopers, rebel adventurers, alien octopoid troopers, planetary striker troops, and two types of robots.

User Summary

A Galacta series game from Heritage USA's Paint 'N' Play line.

The Paint 'N' Play games all featured Heritage miniatures along with paints and painting instructions. Unlike the other Paint 'N' Play games, which featured a board, the Galacta series games were straight miniatures rules designed to be played directly on the tabletop.

The contents included metal miniature figures, paints, a brush, "adventure game rules", and a 12-sided die. Heritage sold additional Galacta 25mm figures that could be used with the games.

From publisher's comprehensive rulebook: 
Heroclix ID cards show that a character on your force has a special contact within a larger organization that may be able to
temporarily help. Many, but not all ID cards say they are “Unique”, and therefore only one ID card with that set symbol and collector number can be added to your force. When adding an ID card to your starting force, you must also add an associated ID character to your Sideline. [ID cards have a cost of 5.]

Here is the text of ID cards:
Inspiration: “A specific effect that varies from ID card to ID card.”
Setup – At the beginning of the game, choose a character on your sideline with a name or real name matching this ID Card and turn it to a starting line. This character is called an ID Character. You can’t have more ID characters on your force than you have characters placed on the map at the beginning of the game. (If the card is Unique, it will say so here.)
Call in Help – At the beginning of your turn, you may activate one ID Card per force. To activate an ID Card, choose a friendly character that has no action tokens and is equal to or more points than the ID Character's starting line. Give the chosen character a power action, place the ID Character adjacent to it, and then remove this ID Card from the game and your opponent scores it. The ID characters can’t be carried, given double power actions, replaced, or assigned resources. The ID character returns to your sideline immediately: (a) at the beginning of your next turn, (b) if it's not within 5 squares of the chosen character, or (c) if it is removed from the map by another game effect.
Inspire – When a character friendly to an ID Character is in an adjacent square, they can use that ID Character's Inspiration.

boardgame: Stop Thief!: Jason L'Mean Promo Card
description:
Promo card given out from the 2017 Dice Tower Indiegogo campaign.

Jason L'Mean - When captured, All other investigators choose and discard a movement card (other than their lowest one) Value: $8,000

THE CLUTCHES OF DEATH BECKON…

An ancient cult of necromancers stirs throughout the darker reaches of western Immoren and bids the dead to rise up against the living. From the crumbling subterranean passages of the Undercity to the swamps of the Widower’s Wood, these masters of death work toward unseen ends more nefarious than any might imagine. Only those brave few willing to face their own mortality can have any hope of stopping the sinister plots unfolding in the shadows. Steel yourself and prepare to send wraiths, boneswarms, and other unspeakable horrors back to the grave!

The Dead Men Walking expansion contains six new scenarios that pit players against hosts of undead servants and their ﬁendish masters. These compelling scenarios can be played on their own or mixed into the larger campaigns of The Undercity and Widower’s Wood.

—description from the publisher

User summary:

The players choose a profession (office worker, laborer, physician, farmer, craftsman, trader) and may take the roles of mayor, cashier, city manager, journalist.
The pawns are moved around the board clockwise. Movement is determined by a die. Some spaces or event cards make the players earn or lose money. Money is needed to gain achievement token by  building muncipal buildings to improve the infrastructure of the city.

When all muncipal buildings are finished, the player with the most achievement token wins the game.

Included on the backside of the game board is the travel game "Österreich-Rundfahrt", where the players have to answer quiz questions about the municipal companies in every city the reach.

Nimbee: The Bee's Knees is a small expansion for the Nimbee  base game that consists of five new bonus cards to swap in to offer a new balance to in-game decisions.

The Bee's Knees deck was developed by BGG users in an August 2017 competition to create new bonus cards.

—description from the publisher

Leap Frog is a game created in New Zealand where opponents pieces are captured by leaping over them. To start each player in turn places one of their four counters in any slot of the nine slots around the circle on the board. Each player in turn then performs a slide move, double jump or jump move, though if the next player can not move, the player who just moved takes another turn. The first player to capture all four of his opponents pieces wins the game.
Taluva Extension is for the basic Taluva game from Ferti, not Taluva Deluxe. It contains the two-hex tiles expansion, the ships expansion, the fifth player expansion, and the game board expansion.

A mini-expansion for Piraten Kapern, released in a promo pack at the "Stadt, Land, Spielt"-Event in Germany and Austria.

This blister contains two sorts of special cards which can be added to the game. Each special card is available 4 times.

The function of the first special card, called "Nach dem Sturm" ("After the storm!), is: You are allowed to roll any amount of dice just 2 times. You are still not allowed to reroll any skulls, but even with more than 3 skulls on first roll, you are allowed to reroll. You are not allowed to go to Skull Island and only receive points for each diamond and each gold coin, but twice the amount.

The second card is "Frieden" ("Peace"): You have to end your turn without any sabre. If you succeed, you get double the points, else you get -1000 points for each sabre, you had rolled and no points for your other rolls. You are allowed to go to Skull Island, but even there you are not allowed to roll sabres.

A robot-themed, cooperative "drawing quiz game".

All players but one draw two "theme cards" and draw a character representing the combination of the words written (i.e. "Dog" and "President") in 30 seconds.

After drawing a character, all "theme cards" are gathered and shuffled. A number of extra cards may be added, depending on player count.

The one player who did not draw that round must select the correct combination that applies to each drawing from among the mixed cards.
Card game with music theme and combines a 3x3 grid, invoking that of tic-tac-toe. 2 players compete to create the patterns required for performing music pieces.

The story for Deities Domination picks up at the end of a great war. The war to regain the 7 realms from the darkness felled many, including the First of the Gods. As the realms recover, the Gods that once fought together now vie against each other to claim the empty throne.

As a player in the game, choose to be one of several Gods from different clans, each with unique powers and abilities. Players direct their tribe to build, battle monsters, and explore new territories. As a God, players can choose to be Benevolent, focusing on building their tribe or giving gifts to other tribes, or Malevolent, striking down the resources and Heroes of their own or that of other tribes.

The game combines elements of 4x with a deck building mechanic. Each game play is designed to be unique, with multiple characters, a modular map, and several actions for each player to choose from each turn. The race to claim the throne is won by the first player to achieve 12 Victory Points. You can choose to expand your tribes through villages and shrines, build a magnificent temple and collect tributes, go the battle route and fight monsters, take a balanced approach or more.

Deities Domination is a versatile game, designed to maximize replayability. While players may have temporary setbacks, no one is eliminated from the game, and the structure of the game allows for recovery from asymmetric distribution of power, giving players the ability to recoup from disadvantageous situations. The combination of new map layouts, Gods’ abilities, and multiple paths to victory creates an inimitable experience each game.

—description from the publisher

Conquer: The Ultimate Drinking Game, is an adult party game that combines strategy board game with drinking. In the game players can take drinks to purchase bonuses for their armies, like extra troops or a shield. Conquer is a game of balance. As player's drink and collect bonuses they also start to lose their mental focus.

—description from the publisher

Players play cards in turn.
Score or steal score.
A player with a score of one hundred points wins.
Five minutes simple game.

boardgame: Gothic: A Dark Companion for In Her Majesty's Name
description:
This is the third supplement for the game and has been produced by The Ministry of Gentlemanly Warfare, authors of IHMN, Daishō and Blood Eagle.

Within these pages, you shall find new weapons and equipment, talents and powers, and creatures both normal and supernatural. As well as a host of new companies to fight or, bring on, the darkness that threatens to engulf the Old World.

You will meet old enemies such as Vlad Tepes, Prince of Wallachia, and his nemesis Abraham van Helsing. Play the brave men of the Imperial Nightwatch as they test their modern might against the terrifying vampire minions of the immortal Countess Bathory. Rouse the Village mob to drive out Baron Viktor von Frankenstein and his abominations. Tread the corridors of power as a member of the Illuminati or, join the Gypsies to hunt Werewolves under the full moon. Witness Sampson Caine and the Nuns of the Order of the Bringer of Salvation defend the innocent from the predations of the Dread Monks of Sucevita. Unleash the Unborn upon the terrible White Slavers. You can also upgrade and test your existing IHMN companies against these many terrors.

New scenarios and landscapes will take your companies into dark forests, mist shrouded castles, ancient ruins, pagan altars and grim, fortified villages the length and breadth of Transylvania, Wallachia and the ever-forbidding Carpathian Mountains.

This is classic Victorian Gothic Horror at its grisly, horripilating best. It is not recommended for ladies and gentlemen of a nervous disposition, for the darkness will feed upon their terror.

—description from the publisher

Note: Gothic is not a stand alone game, and you will need a copy of In Her Majesty’s Name from Osprey publishing in order to play.

description:
じゃんけんノーボーダー (Janken No Border) is a fast 2-player game where each person simultaneously plays one of the illustrated Rock, Paper, Scissors from their hand. The player with the most wins at the end of 5 rounds is the victor.

The biggest feature of this game: the fact that you cannot tell for sure what card (Rock, Paper, or Scissors) you are playing until it’s too late. There are a total of 16 numbered cards and while Card #1 and Card #16 are clearly defined as Rock and Paper respectively, it’s impossible to say what range is defined as Scissors until the end of the game.

After five rounds of placing out your cards, you use the two remaining cards left in your hand to figure out what range of cards actually count as “Scissors” for each individual player — and therefore how many of the five rounds you actually won.

A playful game of psychological warfare that combines funnily illustrated cards with serious strategy as you guess what cards your opponent could possibly have left in his or her hand and play your own hand accordingly.
—user summary

グーやパーなどの手が描かれたカードを同時に伏せて出し、じゃんけんをする２人用ゲームです。じゃんけん勝負を５回行い、勝った回数が多い方のプレイヤーが勝者となります。

このゲームの大きな特徴は、５回の勝負に使うカードをすべて出すまで各カードのグー・チョキ・パーが確定しないことです。最終的にじゃんけん勝負に使わなかったカードによって各カードのグー・チョキ・パーが決まります。
人をからかっているかのような変わったカードの図柄と、相手がどんなカードを残しているのかを推理しながら手札を出していく心理戦が特徴的なゲームです。
—description from the publisher

Engines roar through the night sky as you see clouds of dust coming your way. Hot tires screech across the road as hundreds of riders wreck havoc to anything on their path. They are followed by heavily armored warriors, intimidating everyone with a cacophony of metallic sounds made by their chains, net launchers, and other weapons of post apocalyptic war.

First, the riders are going to surround and entangle you with their web of steel. Melee fighters will follow soon after, bringing pain and destruction. Even if you are going to defend yourself, even if you manage defeat one of them, more replace their fallen comrade.

Death is coming your way. You can try to run. You can try to hide. But sooner or later, you are going to fall into their hands. This is not your average gang – this is Iron Gang, a brutal group that has perfected the art of the hunt from the back of the steel steed.

Neuroshima Hex: Iron Gang introduces a new army pack to Neuroshima Hex!, and both the HQ and certain units feature a new "chain" ability. Chain connects two units with that ability if there is a straight line between them, and it allows them to target and hit any unit on that line.

—description from the publisher

description:
A single promo card for the Top Trumps: Star Wars Rebels deck, released in a promotional package for the "Stadt, Land, Spielt"-Event in Germany and Austrian.

Top Trumps Info (translated from German card):
Tseebo (TM) was a close friend of Ezra Bridger's (TM) parents and was working for the "Imperial Office of Information", from where he escaped, carrying confidential files. He was wanted by "the Empire", but saved by Ezra and "the Rebels".

When he came to the alphabet, Theuth said: "This art, O king, will make the Egyptians wiser and richer in memory." This is the myth of the invention of writing according to Plato’s Phaidros, which MYTH OF THEUTH takes as base for a playful examination of media theories.

In this fantastic game you will move through ancient Athens, collecting different media in order to complete a mission. You will meet famous people who greatly influenced the development of media and cultural theories. Gotthold Ephraim Lessing shows you the limits of the image, Laura Mulvey takes a delightful gaze on your memorizing abilities, and Marshall McLuhan finally gets his well-deserved massage. Across the 12 stations, you will use smartphones, newspapers, stamps, candy, hand, feet, pairs of eyes, and other means to discover the Myth of Theuth. Whoever manages to finish the mission first – with a combination of strategy, luck, and knowledge – will be worshiped as a divine medium!

MYTH OF THEUTH is designed as a board game, which will be available in three different versions in English and German:


     PHAEDRUS, free available version to produce at home, needs approx. 5 to 6 hours;
     PLATON, standard version, first edition of 500 pcs.
     THEUTH, exclusive version, higher quality of used materials, additional game material (own game pieces, paper, pencils, newspaper articles, post-it notes, candies, LEGO bricks, blindfold, liqueur, envelopes, stamps), limited edition of 100 pcs., signed by artists.

Mixed Construction: A sentence that has incompatible elements; the sentence begins one way, with one kind of structure, and then shifts to a different structure.

In this cooperative wordplay game, a team of players works together to create a clue about a person, place or thing given to them from a randomly drawn card. Their partner will be trying to guess what's on their card. Mixed Constructions is a race against the clock where the team works together to maximize the number of correct guesses.

The catch is: players giving the clue can only say one word at a time!
—description from the designer
