description: Expansion for Project Z. Includes 8 plastic miniatures (have to be assembled) and 25 game cards.

Sigils of Ruin is an expansion for Eschaton.

Before the great battle at the end of days, each player chooses one of six special characters to act as champion for their cult deck. This champion will play alongside other cultists, but will influence the overall play style of the deck in a direction defined by the champion’s affiliation.

Partnered with each champion is a corresponding avatar. Through the ritual invocations based on their champion’s affiliation, a cult will eventually be able to summon their avatar to join their deck. These avatars are not only powerful characters, but the direct path to the Sigils of Ruin.

Once the avatar has served their purpose on the mortal plane, they can be sacrificed to channel the cult’s corresponding sigil. When conjured, these sigils are immovable auras that bestow their cult with unique abilities and victory conditions. Although no two paths are the same, they each demand equal favor from the Dark One.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Festival of Thousand Cats: Sakazuki Promo
description: Before setting up the game, add these two special cards into the deck to replace the two cards of the same season number from the basic game. The special cards will change the game end condition.

User summary
マゴス (Magos) is a dice game for one or two people. Battle each other with magic and defeat your opponent.

Each player is a wizard. Choose 5 spells and beat down the opponent's wizard. When it comes to your turn, use 1 to 4 dice and assign and use spells.

The game includes four 6-sided dice, cubes for Hit Points and Mana, and 33 illustrated spell cards. The game is also offered in a Print & Play (PnP) version and online.

Each playes has to get his pawn from "Start" to "Ziel". Movement is determined by one die. The players have to get on every space marked with a letter. From there, the rolled number decides the direction of movement.

Ekklesia is a variant of Careers. The players set their own victory conditions. A player may choose to pursue faith, love, joy or a combination of all three. The total number of "points" earned in the 3 categories must total 50. Money is only a means to achieve these goals.

The players move their pawns arrond the board. Movement is determined by a die. Some spaces events influence the players movement, gains them money or cause them to spend money. Other spaces cause the players to draw a card and follow the instructions printed there. The players try to fulfill their goal by going through any number of different detours. On finishing a detour, the player gets a movement card, that he may use instead of rolling the die at a time of his own choosing.

The player who gets to his pre-set goal first wins the game.

The players start at London airport (center space). Movement is determined by a die. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first player to return to London after concluding the four journeys wins the game.

Tracers is a 1-4 player deck-building board game in which players take on the role of androids after a failed rebellion.    Having their numbers decimated in the android wars, in which they sought their emancipation, they now seek to inject their consciousness into cyberspace and steal the secret to sentient life from the humans in order to replenish their numbers.

The board is comprised of spaces connected by pathways, representing various systems which can be hacked.  Players start with a 12 card deck and a 6 card hand and cooperate to survive while competing for victory points.  They roll dice against their hack points to hack systems and, if successful, get to add better cards to their deck.  If unsuccessful they generate "trace" which may draw enemy A.I. called "hunters" to their location.  If more than one player hacks the same system (i.e. space on the board) the threat of a "tracers" attack grows, tracers being strange and powerful creatures that roam cyberspace.

The goal is to hack two well protected systems and acquire the tokens there which represent the human data, but they will have to have strong hacking abilities and protective gear to overcome that challenge.

THE STORY

Year 2067: global warming is no longer a prophecy but a reality.
The world, as we know it, has changed. Cities, governments, and nationalities got diluted giving way to a single world power, the World Federation. This new order represents few privileged, citizens of former first powers who today live protected from the chaos and misery that invades the planet. The rest of humanity migrates aimlessly on the run from war, hunger, and destruction left behind by climate change.

Meanwhile, the new world, driven by state-of-the-art technology, raises its walls seeking isolation from chaos. Set in the form of Smart Cities and defended by machines, the new world order is forced to find a solution to uncover illegal immigrants who manage to cross its borders and disguise among its citizens. Andrana Project, a Nano chip implanted in the nape of its inhabitants is the new identity document. From this moment on, everything involving a person is linked to their nanochip: their identity, bank and medical details, properties, titles... everything.

Project Andrana is well received by the majority of the population, although many are opposed to its potential risks. In this context, an old ideological movement begins to acquire strength and prominence: The Neoluddites.

Convinced that technology has a negative impact on people, their communities and the environment, Neoluddites believe that social and ecological collapse condemns most of the world's population to live in misery. Although this ideology has been alive for years, its followers had just passively abandoned technology until the arrival of Project Andrana, after which they started to get organized to act as a group. Neoluddites are dispersed all over the world and they know no frontiers. There are different factions, some more pacifist, and other more radical ones, determined to end the hegemony of the World Federation, which has taken technological evolution to the extreme. In time, some citizens of the World Federation will join this movement, tearing their nanochip off, becoming exiles and preparing to fight the system.
 Constant confrontations between the World Federation and the Neoluddites will shape the fate of humanity.

THE GAME

This is the plot of Andrana Project, an independent game that will be able to test your strategic ability, emotional intelligence, teamwork capacity and leadership skills.

Andrana Project is a phygital social game, this means that it is a group game, played face to face, supported on your smart phone or tablet.

To play Andrana Project you need a minimum of 4 players with smartphone/tablet and Internet connection. Every player needs to download the game, one of them will start the game and each of them will be assigned a team and a character.

In Andrana Project there are two opposing teams: the Neoluddites and the World Federation. Players from both teams have to discover their opponents to shut them down. The game has different missions that will get upgraded depending on how much it’s played.

Each player will be assigned a different character from each team, which means also different abilities and power inside the game.

These are the main powers of each of them (among many others):

The Neoluddites leader: As the Leader, he can recruit citizens. If he recruits a citizen he/she will become a Neoluddite and will fight for his team.

Neoluddites Doctor: As a Doctor, she can heal herself or any player she wants, so she can prevent that person of dying.

Neoluddites Citizen: As a citizen, they can save someone’ life, giving theirs instead. If they save someone from their team will result in a lot of points.

All Neoluddites have to be careful when stealing the access. If they steal the wrong person their identity will be revealed to that player.

Cybersecurity: As Cybersecurity, they can spy on one player to know his/her identity. They have the module, the Andrana server’s access. The Neoluddites want to steal them. The Agent knows who this person is and he will try to protect him.

DCS Agent: A DCS Agent can arrest other players to disable them in the next round. They are the only one who can recharge their weapon and attack more than once.
They must protect Cybersecurity, being aware of shooting him, if they kill him their team loses the game.

CITIZEN: As a citizen, they can save someone’s life, giving theirs instead. If they save someone from their team will result in a lot of points.

Each player needs to hide its identity from the rest, and at the same time observe their opponents to uncover theirs.

Cypress Legacy: The Board Game is for 2 to 6 players taking place in one of the Cypress Mansions. Players will take on the role of trying to work their way through the massive mansion while trying to achieve their objectives, and prove to be Lorna's true friend. Each player knows of the other players that are within the mansion and views them as someone who is trying to steal what is there. The player to achieve the goals, will be considered the winner and thus the true friend of Lorna. You will have at times, skills that you can earn or possibly steal. You can also acquire assets and or weapons that will aid you in your task at hand.

Classified Prototypes are very hard to get but are extremely useful if acquired. One example of a Classified Prototype is being able to gain control over the Knights for one turn, be able to capture as well as then send adversaries to the Storage Room and be locked in.

The mansion has its own elements that are going to be hard to get around but with how it has been designed there are also a few very useful elements it also offers. One such benefit is the Security access key, that if acquired gives you additional passages to expedite your journey. Using these and planning your moves well are very important to locate and acquire the "Inheritances". Once achieved then your next task is going to be getting all required items to get out without losing what you have and is needed to take back to Lorna.

Each time a player has a turn, they will draw and event card prior to their role. This will effect their planning and next move or in some cases change everything with the fact the Mansion, Vonya and the Knights are a ever present factor. There are (158) Event Cards Alone.

Additionally in a players turn they can choose to have PVP prior to drawing or roll of dice, depending on cards you may hold and where the other player may be.

Your movement, planning and deck building, will be key to your acquiring what you need and escaping the mansion! (327) Total Cards In Cypress Legacy: The Board Game

Whether you are two players or six, from the first roll to success, the drama to acquire what you need and escape grows continually. This feature is in the game every time you play and with the design, also gives great replayability.

It has taken some time and a lot of changes especially over the last year and a half, but we do now finally have our first run in and are also taking in dealers now. The game has undergone a lot of revisions and art changes. We wanted to take the extra time to adjust the game play and have a very good balance to the game. Now we are very happy with the results and are beginning to get the game out. (Please note this is not a "Legacy" game, this is part of the title because of the overall story.)

Cypress Legacy: The Board Game is based on the Cypress Inheritance Saga.
 OBJECTIVE: Use Weapons, Skills, Assets, and Classified Prototypes to be the first player to escape the Cypress Mansion with all the required inheritance items and be Lorna’s proven friend.

STORY: The story you are playing within begins with Lorna Ritten just learning of Alfred Cypress, the grandfather she has never known. Lorna has always wanted to find out more about her biological family, which has always been a mystery. Her grandfather’s attorney, Phillip Brandt, tells Lorna of an inheritance, which includes one of her grandfather’s many mansions and its contents. Her desire to know more marks the beginning of a very unique and potentially dangerous situation. Lorna has no idea just how important this actually is to her and will be to others. As Lorna is studying some of the inheritance documents before visiting the mansion, she realizes there are objects that hold key information hidden throughout the mansion.

Also note the complete Cypress Legacy: The Board Game Manual in the files below. (Note the player versus player elements in the manual)

The Novel Series has gone through a lot of changes as well and the first novel will be available soon.

—description from the publisher

Your moment has arrived. Gather followers and establish the town to seize the recognition you deserve. Your journey won’t be simple. Will you uphold your honor, or will you succumb to temptation and live a life of dishonor? The choice is yours. What path will you take to earn Renown?

In Renown: The Card Game, you inhabit the role of a unique Personality from the town of Gloria’s Landing. Your ultimate goal: to attain fame and power. You'll gather Followers, venture on Quests, and scheme against your Rivals. As word of your deeds spread, your reputation will grow. The choices you make, for better or worse, will chart your path to Renown! In this fantasy based card game each turn players will play followers, actions, and use abilities to gain Renown. Afterward, they may go on quests where success or failure determines their fate. Play continues around the table, vying for position, until one player reaches their Renown goal.

Together with the Pummeleinhorn and his friends, players of Pummeleinhorn: Das Kartenspiel want to plan the fluffiest parties of the year! The ingredients: party hats, cookies and glitter — lots of glitter!

Whoever arranges three different parties first wins, and to do this, players collect Pummeleinhorn cards until they can put together a suitable combination and fulfill the task of a party card. Will you attempt to plan a cookie-munching party for your friends, or will you dare attempt the legendary magical karaoke party?
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Twin Stars: Adventure Series I – Scenario 06: Bounty Hunt
description:
Two cosmic adventurers are in a tricky scenario and must work together. By rolling and manipulating dice for special abilities and combos, you can be the one that guides them to victory.

The four planets of the Tetran System light up the display as your ship drops out of hyperspace.

"Remember, the mark came here for a reason. Every misstep we make is an opportunity for him to get ready for our arrival."

Now, should we split up, or stick together?"

The bounty has been tracked to the risky Tetran Sector. Use resources to capture the bounty to win. Lose if either character loses all health.

Bounty Hunt is a scenario in the Twin Stars solo system by Jason Tagmire and Mike Mullins. The game consists of just 3 cards and players will provide their own dice and coins/tokens. Each scenario comes with 2 all-new characters and can be interchanged with others for a very different gaming experience.

This scenario has only been released separately as Print & Play, but was then included in Twin Stars: Adventure Series I.

Chronicle of Kings is set during the Viking age (700-1000) in the Fate of the Norns universe. Players take on a dynasty of Viking rulers who wish to be immortalized in legend.

Victory is achieved by being the bloodline with the most renown, and the game rewards players for memorable play. There are many ways to achieve renown, allowing players to pursue different paths to victory.

Every turn the players perform many actions, which include: quests, military conquests, trade/raid, diplomatic intrigue, etc. Each turn spans several years, meaning that the bloodline will need to plan for marriage and children. Mechanics are designed to tell a vivid and memorable story.

The world is not static, and every turn events occur necessitating that sometimes the player work together to overcome a shared challenge.

The game also features a campaign book, allowing for solitaire play.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Binatang is a big strategy game for the whole family. Unchartered territories, inexplicable wild animals and a secret path will be on the table for your family evenings and your time with friends.

What is this game about?

Each player needs to create a sanctuary and fill it with various wild animals separating the territory between different kinds. There is a choice to be made: do you get the rarest animals or do you get as many as possible of  a common kind? Choose your strategy! Each inhabitant in your sanctuary gets you closer to the win! The winner is the one who moves further than others along the secret path.

What’s unique about this game?

The player fills the game space with various animals according to his own plan. And at the same time each move of any other player can change this tactic. Do you need a strategy or you just rely on luck? To focus on creating your own sanctuary or to try influencing the plans of others? Stick to your own style and remember, that even the youngest players have a good chance to outmaster seasoned strategists!

Who is this game for?

Family time with kids
    Kids parties
    Teenagers

—description from the publisher

Special Ops #7 has eight articles, two ASL scenarios, two ASLSK scenarios, and Autumn For Barbarossa, a complete Standard Combat Series (SCS) game.

description:
Fighting in the arena continues. Day by day, oil flows like water, metal groaning as the sound of gunshots spreads across dozens of kilometers along the empty streets of the city.

The Steel Arena: Arsenal expansion changes the basic rules of the game, offering players four new game modes and providing new attack modules and terrain tiles. Now you can speed up your robots, start the game with extra munition, play with new special abilities that are different for each robot, or add some explosive power to your game using new mine tiles!

You can use all four game variants together or combine them in any way you like.

The idea of the game is to fly the planes across no-man's land, into enemy territory and bomb enemy buildings or ammunition dumps and at the same time protect property within own territory from being destroyed by enemy air raids.  The planes start from the first row, move into a formation giving the best advantage, cross no-man's land where they Weill be met by opponents' planes in an attempt to resist the attack.  Planes will be shot down and taken from the board, bombs dropped and buildings destroyed.  In the end the game will be won by the General of the air.
—description from the rulebook

The game includes fighters which can move straight up to 3 spaces or move less and turn.  Bombers can move straight 2 spaces or less and turn.

Supports up to 4 players with 4 sets of planes.

Each of the up to 4 players has to move (options as in Halma) 6 of the 10 pawns to the opposite side of the board. Once a player successfully reaches the goal zone with one pawn the center disc of the game board is rotated 90° left or right. The first player to bring 6 pawns to the goal zone wins.

Dirty Diction is the new Adult Party Game! Have fun! Laugh at your friends! Dirty Diction is the new, hilarious, and slightly educational, party game of idioms, phrases and slang! There are eight different play challenges to get your team to be the first to five points! Act it out! Draw it! Vote on which player is most likely associated with it! Set players up like human mannequins! Do whatever it takes to beat the competition! Watch out though! ‘All In’ challenges could put you ahead or take away your lead! Liven up your game night with Dirty Diction!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Perdition's Mouth: Abyssal Rift – Gamer's Bundle Edition
description:
Perdition's Mouth: Abyssal Rift is intended to be a new kind of dungeon crawl, one that embraces the European school of game design with clever and elegant mechanisms and emphasizes strategy while minimizing luck. Cooperation, planning, and the enemy response deck eliminate the need for chucking dice, creating a tense combat environment with meaningful decisions! The rondels provide another tactical layer, including the enemy's devious and challenging AI.

The rules allow you to learn to play in 15 minutes, with a typical game round lasting less than ten minutes, even with up to six players, but deep, strategic cooperation will be required for your band of heroes to make it through to the Abyssal Rift and maybe even come out alive.

Perdition’s Mouth is designed to be played as a story-supported campaign over several gaming sessions. In the campaign, you have to fight through at least six increasingly challenging scenarios and the level of success during each adventure has a direct effect on the rest of the story. Can you survive the labyrinth of the insectoid god?

Perdition’s Mouth: Abyssal Rift Gamer’s Bundle edition contains all and everything the Retail edition does along with two mini-expansions, Hideout and Witch’s Grotto.

boardgame: Mars Attacks: The Miniatures Game – Martian Grunts
description: Composed of 1 Martian commander, 1 Martian fighter with freezing ray, 8 Martian fighters with disintegrating rifles

6th century ... Saint-Benoît has just finished writing his rule. He describes in 73 chapters how the life of the monks should be managed. Here comes the time of the monastic orders. They will organize themselves and seek to develop their influence.

In Pelegrinus, you lead one of them, launch yourself into the spiritual conquest of Europe. Consolidate your influence by building commandies across the continent, then erect powerful abbeys to assert your power in different regions. Do not forget the intrigues of the papal courts, for the support of the sovereign pontiff will often be valuable to you. Lastly, gather under your protection various relics and maintain a firm control over the lucrative pilgrimage routes. However, you are not alone; other orders intrigue in the shadows to double you on the path of power.

—description from the publisher

Each player is in charge of a different color group of houses in the neighborhood.This game uses territory control and "Take-that!" game mechanics. Every time The Beast is moved to a different house or is annoyed by his neighbors, he gets angrier and angrier. The cards present methods of moving The Beast or rearranging the layout of houses in the neighborhood, and have different anger values for each such method. When The Beast's total anger value reaches the breaking point, he RAGES and destroys all houses adjacent to his.

Each player must manage when and where The Beast rages, thus destroying the houses around him. The ultimate goal is to have more of your opponents' houses destroyed than yours. When The Beast lives in one of your houses, you actually gain points. When The Beast lives next to one of your houses, there's risk of him destroying them in a fit of rage, costing you points. All other houses out of harm's way (not adjacent to The Beast) tally point values as well. The player with the most points wins the game.

description:
Will you be able to pick up the best group of these monstrous diners to spell with them the nicest portions of Midnight Brunch?

Players every turn should try to get the best score by adding the different values ​​of their Monster cards, possibly by helping with the effects of some special cards.

Using an betting mechanic, everyone will have to point to the food they want to eat. But you have to evaluate your score well and try to guess the opponent's hand: as they bluffing or are they really strong?

Will it be better to focus on substandard foods and risk being left dry or having little or no rest?
Who will be more skillful and lucky at the end of 6 rounds of banqueting, will be the winner and "ladies of the night" !!

Opale Noir is a game where you play as precious stones hunters. 
Your goal is to acquire a collection of stones among the rarest and most prized of the planet: Alexandrites, Benitoites, Jadeites, Painites.
In this game all the moves are allowed; you will have recourse to the crime, to the magic of the stones (in other words Lithomancy), to the political manipulations of the Metropoles, to negotiation and other subterfuges to reach your goal.

Players with the most Prestige points win the game.

The mining empires can quickly ascend and be of a formidable power, but here all power is more or less an illusion and many empires have only a fear, see their inconsistency unraveled, end up disfigured by some birds of prey until the fragments of their formerly imposing forces eventually turn against them. Bluff, audacity and deterrence are essential compensating factors for the success of such empires.
It would be sage to mention that these empires share their intrinsic apprehension with the clarity, ruthlessly attract players fanatic of the order, suspecting and nosing without intransigence or even players without morals that such parasites survive to them.
Let us mention again the Lithomanian players who under the blindness of their autarkic devellopement bring to their entourage the severe weight of their misdeeds and spells.
zEach puzzle of these great strategic currents offers their pieces, free to each of them to arrange on the woof of an original and inventive strategy.
We are dealing here with a fierce competition for limited resources, whose pecuniary values and interest in terms of objective correspond to a tight and fluctuating timing.
Within this latent tension it is up to the players to contain their disconveniences because expansion and margins of actions are amply dependent on a collaboration of good quality, sometimes forced, if not imposed.

- The Prestige, deep strategy
Like a game of chess the order is reversed here, the beginning of the game wide open, the abundance and the adaptability of the choices (however tempered by some pecuniary need
and the always too measured coming of the stones) nourish the naiveté of the player, alas for the most part the net does not close too quickly, the voices of survival is reduced and the actions which there will be no need for an interest in an andean are gradually taking on a disproportionate importance.
The players will end up breathless, until the last final moment, but is it not out of breath that it builds the prestige of a victory?

The mini expansion includes 3 new Alexander's generals in the game:

- Seleucus (player mat, special ability tile and conquest point token)

- Perdiccas (player mat, special ability tile and conquest point token)

- Lysimcachus (player mat, special ability tile and conquest point token)

1-2 player expansion for NewSpeak.

For the 1-player component of NewSpeak: Dissoance, players take the role of a solitary Moderator, looking over a conversation between two suspected dissidents, which you must decode using the Moderator Code Cards and Moderator Dashboard as you would in the core game.

Incorrectly determining the meeting location of dissidents will lead to penalties as well as additional clues as you progress through a narrative campaign.

For the 2-player component of NewSpeak: Dissonance, players adopt the personas of two dissidents, attempting to communicate with each other to meet in secret without falling foul of the watchful gaze of the ModNet. Players each have access to incomplete information, which they must use in tandem by communicating using a limited set of coded messages to coordinate their movements.
publishers: Inside the Box Board Games LLP (ITB)

description:
Small World: Sky Islands introduces seven new races and powers to the Small World base game, but it also gives those races — and all the previously released races — new territory in which to fight for control.

At the start of play, place the Sky Islands game board so that it shows either two or three islands in the sky (your choice), then use the Small World game board as if you were playing with one fewer player, i.e., use the four-player board when playing with five players. Next, place access points to the Sky Islands — the beanstalk and the stairway — on different regions on the game board. Whenever a race stands on one of these access points, they can try to conquer the space on the sky islands that shows the matching symbol.

Races can't start their conquests in the Sky Islands unless their power specifically allows them to do so. At the end of a turn, if you control all of the regions on a Sky Island, you gain one additional coin.

boardgame: Meade's Turn: The Mine Run Campaign and Gettysburg
description:
Two division level American Civil War games. Uses the same system as the designer's previous At All Hazards game with two changes plus additional game specific rules.

Corps, Cavalry, and Supply wagons are activated by chit-pull. Each unit on the map is a Division with off map tracking boxes for supply, fatigue, and stragglers for each Division.  Divisions have between one and four steps. Turns represent 3 1/2 hours and map hexes are approx. 1/2 mile across.

Gettysburg is won on geographical objectives with step losses counting, too. Mine Run is won solely on the basis of damage to the enemy force.

Only a playtest version produced.  The designer also produced an experimental order system inspired by the Gamers' CWB (but without writing).

description:
Dinomals: the action packed, mix and match, create a creature card game made by kids for kids!

Your goal: make a Dinomal!

Dinomals are half dinosaur and half modern day animal. Get the right dinosaur card and animal card and smush them together to make your Dinomal before your opponents. You can also mess with your opponents hand to stop them from building a Dinomal with SLAM cards!

—description from the publisher

In an effort to explain what the Smart Microgrid Applied Research Team (SMART group) does at the British Columbia Institute of Technology, Alan Stewart designed the SMART Microgrid Card Game.  It's a fast, easy-to-play "competition" to see who can manage their RESEARCH in order to be the first to build their Microgrid technologies in all three categories: GRID, ENERGY, and PEOPLE, all while trying to deal with DELAYs played by their opponent.

In gamer terms, it's an anti-deck-building fighting game.

A group of mice must navigate a giant pyramid of cheese, racing each other to see who can collect the most before reaching the top. Mice can wiggle through the holes in the cheese to collect even more, but watch out for the cat!

Having found a lot of holes  in the cheese, the pussy howls: “Mice must feel too much at ease, eating up the master’s cheese. I will guard it night and day,  shooing naughty mice away!” Careful, mice - the cat is sly,  it will hit you – you will fly. Go running to the top! Keep  the cheese and do not stop! Traps are no threat with cheese, win, do not be scared of these!

GAME COMPONENTS: 
Cheese pyramid – 1,  
‘cheese lumps’ 
with holes – 4, 
 numbered tokens – 25, 
 chips:  wooden mouse — 4,  
wooden cat — 1;  
glass balls – 4, 
dice – 1, 
 rules —  1.

THE PURPOSE OF THE GAME:
To be first to reach the cheese pyramid, and gain as many points as possible!

THE PROGRESS OF THE GAME
 •  The game participants take turns to throw the dice and move the mice game pieces towards the top of the cheese pyramid along the tiers (levels). • The game pieces may only be moved anti-clockwise.    • The movement to each subsequent tier (level) is via four staircases. • Only one game piece may be located in the field. Game pieces may “step over” each other. If the relevant field is  occupied by another game piece, the player should go to  the free field ahead of it.  • On the fields with symbols the players must comply with their conditions (see the playing field symbols). • When moving or “stepping” with a game piece over other game pieces the players must hold the marble with a finger of only one hand.

PLAYING FIELD SYMBOLS 
1.  The mouse game piece stopped in the field with this symbol: • If the player does not have the “holes” of his/her piece of cheese closed, he/she will take one token from the bank, and cover the holes with it blindfold, i. e., without turning the token with the number upwards. • If all the holes are already covered with tokens, the player will make an additional move. 
2.  The cat game piece has stopped on this field, so the move is to be made to the player who is next in turn. • If the player’s game piece gets onto the field with the cat’s symbol, the player will throw the dice and move the cat’s game piece by the number of points shown on the dice.  See special cases that may occur during the cat’s movement  in VI and VII. 
3. If the game piece gets into this field, the marble will fall into the hole and roll down to the other tier (level).  The player will move the mouse game piece to a new field, cover the marble with it again, and take two tokens from the bank and cover the “holes” on the cheese piece with them.  See special cases in IV and V. NOTE. The trap field will be inactive if all five holes on the cheese piece are closed with tokens. In this case the player will put the mouse game piece near the hole, so as prevent the marble from falling. 
4. On the upper tier the Players’ game pieces will be moving in a circle until one of them (or the cat game piece) stands precisely on the field with a red flag — the finish field. NOTE. For the smallest kids the game may be finished when the first player reaches or crosses the field with the flag. • Then the players turn their cheese pieces upside down and count the points on the tokens. The one who gains most points will be the winner.  • In the event of two players with equal number of points the one who has reached the finish or is closer to the same will win.

Developing game "Cat and Mouse" in the form of a dimensional pyramid develops fine motor skills, memory and attention. Gives an idea of ​​numbers and scoring. Trains accurate eye. Unique 3D form of the pyramid allows you to play not only in one-dimensional plane but in three-dimensional space.
—description from the publisher

The object of the game is to be the first to get your mouse to the top of the pyramid while avoiding the cat.

Each player controls a wooden mouse covering a marble. Players take turns rolling the die and moving their mouse. Some spots allow the marble to drop, requiring the player to move their mouse to that lower location. Other spots trigger the cat to move.
—user summary

Peninsula is a 2-player asymmetrical strategy card game capturing the tension of the 2017 North Korea Crisis. One player controls North Korea, the other the United States. As the crisis unfolds, players will try to use a combination of diplomacy and military posturing in a bid to claim victory without the situation devolving into a conflict that could leave both sides in ruins.

Players attempt to reduce their opponent's resolve and control regional tension by playing diplomacy and progress cards. When tension rises above certain levels, players receive more progress cards and the crisis develops, yet these cards are the most powerful way to reduce opposition resolve. Each decision could have far-reaching consequences and the challenge is to claim victory in the crisis without both sides destroying each other.

—description from the designer

Everything that was old is new again. Sovereign Sight is the first Data Pack from the "Kitara Cycle" for Android: Netrunner, and its sixty cards (three copies each of twenty different cards) transport the game's Corps and Runners to the shores of Lake Victoria, where the nations of the Sub-Saharan League are gambling everything on their ability to create a second beanstalk.

The first Data Pack to release following the 2017 rotation, Sovereign Sight sets the course for the new Android: Netrunner metagame, focusing on bluffs, gambits, and tense interactions between Corp and Runner.

—description from the publisher

Portals act as in-game accelerants: when you pay the Rune cost of the Portal, you begin drawing cards from the center deck until you draw a Hero or Construct that is within two Runes of the Portal's cost. For example, if you purchase a 4-cost Portal, it could quickly turn into a 6-cost Emri, One with the Void.

Portals are a great way to generate fun, memorable moments when you're playing with your friends and family.

BuddySays is a friendship-provoking game- A simple, fun and easy to play the game; BuddySays focuses on trolling, pranking and social interaction as its themes.

Designed with 2 Gameplay modes; Core and Alternate - BuddySays uses the point system to determine the winner. Integrating mobile phone; specifically on making calls as the main method in Core Mode, while playing with other players on the table in Alternate; - making BuddySays a potential super fun and engaging party game.

Get your buddies to say weird stuff with BuddySays. Made for people who are in a relationship with their mobile phones. And trolling buddies.

description: In Attack on Dol Guldur, players join the host of Lórien in a direct attack against Sauron’s Mirkwood stronghold taken straight from the appendix for The Return of the King. description: This is a set of nine cards for Superfight that was released at PAX West 2017. Each swag bag contained 1 random card.

Ito Itsunagi is the eldest of the heirs to the Ito clan. His training has been second to none and his skill at arms is quite astounding. A brutal and coldblooded killer, his only downfall is his arrogance, mocking opponents with his lack of armour.

A priestess of Orochi, she has dedicated her life to the snake kami and has been favoured by beginning the transformation. With the merest of looks Sakura can corrupt a man, turning the blood running through his veins into poison.

How the transformation will take hold no one can be sure, Akimoto is taking on the very image of Orochi. In the heat of the battle his allies claim he transforms further, this claim is supported by the opponents that flee in terror in the face of his onslaught.

Chiyo has distinguished herself further from her fellow Temple Bushi, she quickly mastered the art of the Asp Strike and her spirit and determination to return to the fray when knocked down has been noted by her superiors.

Abyssal Empress expansion includes:


     1 Abyssal Empress model
     10 Abyssal Empress action cards
     1 Abyssal Empress squad card
     5 additional scenarios

You're going on a trip in Kofferpacken extrem, and you want to pack your suitcase with many things. Who can invent the most creative items with the letters given? And can you remember what everyone else has packed?

boardgame: Runewars Miniatures Game: Darnati Warriors – Unit Expansion
description:
The enigmatic swordsmen of the Latari emerge from the deepest reaches of the Deepwood with the Darnati Warriors Unit Expansion for Runewars Miniatures Game! These elven warriors turn combat into a deadly dance, using their moonstone blades to slice through enemies with a speed unmatched in Terrinoth. Master the blade and defend the Aymhelin!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Runewars Miniatures Game: Wraiths – Unit Expansion
description:
Fear is made flesh with the Wraiths Unit Expansion for Runewars Miniatures Game! With the ability to pass through walls and unleash a shriek to terrify even the most stalwart Daqan warriors, these phantoms torment the armies of the living with an unnatural fury. Unleash the ancient specters and make the living cower before you!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Runewars Miniatures Game: Flesh Rippers – Unit Expansion

The army of the Uthuk Y'llan unleash their fiendish cavalry with the Flesh Rippers Unit Expansion for Runewars Miniatures Game! These swift creatures rend flesh from bone, smelling the blood in the hearts of their enemies even at great distances. Expand your locust swarm and terrorize Terrinoth!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Runewars Miniatures Game: Outland Scouts – Unit Expansion
description:
At the farthest reaches of Terrinoth, remote villages find their protectors in the Outland Scouts Unit Expansion for Runewars Miniatures Game! These inspirational soldiers use their knowledge of the land to outwit invaders and rally the greater Daqan forces with their indomitable faith. Call your banners and defend Terrinoth at all costs!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Runewars Miniatures Game: Berserkers – Unit Expansion
description:
The infantry of the locust swarm takes to the battle eld with the Berserkers Unit Expansion for Runewars Miniatures Game! Hailing from the steppes of the Ru Darklands, these howling tribesmen strike fear into their enemies as they attack without reason or mercy. Grow your Uthuk army and bring Terrinoth to its knees!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Runewars Miniatures Game: Kethra A'laak – Hero Expansion
description:
The most terrifying witch of the blood coven takes her command with the Kethra A'laak Hero Expansion Pack for Runewars Miniatures Game! Feed you blood lust and conquer the realm for the locust swarm!

boardgame: Runewars Miniatures Game: Spined Threshers – Unit Expansion
description:
Call forth the greatest demons of the Ynfernael with the Spined Threshers Unit Expansion for Runewars Miniatures Game! These grotesque monsters raze villages and can tear down castle walls with ease. Destroy your enemies and make mortals tremble with fear before you!

This learning game consists of cardboard rectangles containing pictures of animals and the traces they leave behind when eating. For example, a slug and a mushroom with bites taken out of it.

There are two sets included:

     one set of two-part black & white cardboard rectangles: one square shows the animal, the other the traces they leave behind when eating
     one set of pairs of colored cardboard squares of just the animals
     one set of pairs of colored cardboard squares of just the traces


The rules describe how to play dominoes with the black & white pieces, and a couple of variations of memory with the colored pieces.

–user summary

A game covering the Sekigahara campaign all over Japan.

The game system is derived from A Few Acres of Snow by Martin Wallace.
Each player starts the game with their own card deck worth 10VPs.  Each turn, players can purchase new cards worth their current VP total.
Most clans starts the game inactive, and players need to purchase the cards representing them. They also mobilize, move, and attack with cards.
The game lasts 8 turns.  Victory is decided by controlling VP areas or killing the opposing leader.

Unit scale: 2,000-3,000 men per counter
Game period: Keichō 5
Counters: 198
Map: A2 (42 x 59.4 cm)
Cards: 32


Published in Game Journal #64.

boardgame: Ashes Reborn: The Goddess of Ishra

A new Phoenixborn enters the fray with The Goddess of Ishra expansion deck for Ashes: Rise of the Phoenixborn! All enemies kneel before Astrea, who provides enlightenment to all who seek her out. Spring into action with this full, ready-to-play deck, or use the cards you find here to build your own deck centered on the Phoenixborn of your choice. With each Ashes expansion deck, you can supplement your collection with new spell and unit cards in order to play out all-new battles!

In addition to Koji Wolfcub, The Boy Among Wolves includes new spells, units, and conjurations that give you the freedom to create your own combinations of cards and explore the world of Ashes like never before!

boardgame: Star Wars: Armada – Chimaera Expansion Pack
description:
Grand Admiral Thrawn arrives to the battles of Star Wars: Armada in the Chimaera Expansion Pack. Along with the chance to command your fleet with this brilliant, blue-skinned Chiss tactician, the Chimaera Expansion Pack introduces an Imperial Star Destroyer adorned with a distinctive chimaera design, two Mandalorian Gauntlet squadrons, six ship cards, and seventeen other upgrades. Altogether, these materials don't just give your Star Destroyer a different aesthetic; they allow it to fill entirely new tactical and strategic roles within your Imperial fleet.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Armada – Profundity Expansion Pack
description:
With its powerful ship and cunning commander, the Profundity Expansion Pack for Star Wars: Armada lends new hope to the rag-tag Rebel fleet. Its miniature pre-painted MC75 star cruiser boasts an impressive array of weapons, armor, and shields, and it comes with two ship cards and fourteen upgrades that feature Admiral Raddus, Jyn Erso, and other notable characters from Rogue One. Together, these crew members and the Profundity Title upgrade allow you to make a wide range of clever adjustments to your overall strategy.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – Alpha-Class Star Wing Expansion Pack
description:
The first widely deployed Imperial star fighter to boast shields and a hyperdrive, the Alpha-class Star Wing had limited maneuverability, but was heavily armed for its size. The fighter remains impressively durable and destructive in Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game, where its two agility, four hull, and three shields are paired with the ability to unleash cascading torrents of missile and cannon fire. The Star Wing's pre-painted miniature in Alpha-Class Star Wing Expansion Pack is accompanied by four ship cards, seven upgrades, one maneuver dial, reference cards for the reload action and jam tokens, and all the plastic and tokens you need to fly the ship to battle.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – M12-L Kimogila Fighter Expansion Pack
description:
Produced by MandalMotors and flown by enforcers of the Hutt kajidics and other criminal cartels, the M12-L Kimogila Fighter was dreaded by spacers across the Outer Rim for its ordnance capacity and the withering torrents of coordinated fire it could unleash. The M12-L Kimogila Fighter Expansion Pack brings this heavy fighter to life in Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game as a blunt instrument that trades maneuverability for raw destructive power. Its pre-painted miniature star fighter comes with four ship cards and ten upgrades, many of which reward you for catching your foes in the ship's new bullseye firing arc.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – Phantom II Expansion Pack
description:
The Phantom II is a Sheathipede-class transport shuttle that the Spectres recovered from a Clone Wars-era military base and modified with weapons and a custom docking system. It makes its Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game debut in the Phantom II Expansion Pack as an attack-ready vessel that comes with four ship cards and six upgrades. These include new astromechs, crew upgrades, and Title upgrades that incorporate more of Star Wars Rebels in your space battles and add depth to the shuttle's ability to perform the coordinate action.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

description:
Fruit Ninja: Card Master is a fast-paced deck building game that allows 2 to 4 players to compete and prove their wit and dexterity. As ninja apprentices, they need to master their arts and prove to Sensei who is the most worthy trainee.

The game takes place over four rounds in which each player has to play their 15-card deck in a real-time match-making showdown. After each battle, players have a chance to upgrade their cards and improve their deck!

—description from the publisher

A Kickstarter campaign was initiated for a series of 3 games based on the popular app game. Having not met the expected goals, the campaign was terminated. From the 3 games announced, only 1 made it to print: Fruit Ninja: Combo Party.

This expansion works perfectly well with Core (not Centauri Saga: Abandoned)

The expansion adds 2 more players so now you can play the scenarios with 5 and 6 players.

The Arena expansion introduce four(4) competitive scenarios Player vs Player  and one (1) new Co-op Scenario (Reaching for the stars).
—description from the publisher

On these comic-book-game you are the hero (or the zombie).
Each frame has options to go further in history as a survivor in an zombie outbreak trying to survive and scape the city But if you like you can play as an infected zombie trying to eat and scape the city.
Although the main objective for both characters are the same, the gameplay and the possibilities for each as unique, flowing through the book.
2 Game-books in one.

Board game based on the 1959 / 1960 U.S. TV Detective series.

From the instructions:

Johnny Staccato, a piano player at Waldo's Club is also a private eye with 6 special cases on his books which involve :-
Murder / Arson / Blackmail / False Pretences / Burglary / Hit and Run Road Accident.
He enlists the aid of his friends, each choosing a case to solve. A friendly rivalry exists between them as they each endeavour to collect a set of clues that will solve a case.

OBJECT OF THE GAME:

The object of the game is to be the first to collect a complete set of 8 cards of one particular series which will show the complete crime from start to finish. The first player to do so is the winner.

boardgame: Black Lion's Roar: The Battle of Cantigny, May 27-31, 1918
description:
During their Spring offensive, the German 82nd Reserve Division captured the crucial crossroads town of Catigny. The newly arrived 1st US Infantry Division was rushed to the front and its 28th Regiment, nicknamed the Black Lions, was ordered to retake and hold the town. Although the Americans quickly took the town from the disorganized and ill-prepared defenders, the Black Lions had to then endure days of increasingly desperate and violent counter-attacks that decimated the ranks of both sides. The outcome of the battle would be a telling indicator of America’s new role in the Great War.
—description from the publisher

In a wild west were adventurers, outlaws and citizens are addicted and powered by coffee, a devastating plague threatens to end once and for all the storages of the precious black gold. Now, the last 3 beans are traveling in a reinforced train in order to be planted in a safe soil, but not without dragging the attention of everybody across the railroad.

In this 2 / 4 players game, you must fight as an Authority or an Outlaw to keep or steal the last 3 fertile coffee beans in the State.

Each character has its own set of actions printed in transparent cards, which are inserted in a bigger card with the opponent's actions as well, creating the base game cards. Then, each player must chose a card and play it at the same time. The person with the initiative executes his or her action first, then the other does this as well. After that, cards are swapped. It means the Authority's card goes to the Outlaw and vice-versa.

The Outlaw must then use these actions to move alongside the train to steal the beans before it arrives its destination, and, off course, without losing all his or her health points. Meanwhile, the Authority must keep at least one bean until the final station. The time track advances everytime the cards are swapped out.

Fight to save, or maybe even steal, the last hope of this coffee-driven civilization in this quick and strategic game by Potato Cat.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A promo card of a viking visiting the village of Blackwood.

You can play the viking like a Potion card. If you  give him 2 four-leaf clover, you will win 6 coins and the viking will return in the North Sea.

The Scroll is a deck building game where players compete against each others, and against the game itself. Players incarnate heroes with unique abilities, expanding the powers on their quest to collect the Tears of Power - the artefacts needed for the ritual summoning the Scroll - and become the ruler of the Sacred Lands."

Each player starts the game with a hero (with unique powers to use through the game), a starting deck of crystals (the in-game currency), and a secret quest. Each hero has also a specific thematic and colour, corresponding to the type of crystals they use.

Four card stacks - one for each corresponding crystal colour - are used to get new cards. Players start only buying cards from their own crystal colour, until they gain new abilities allowing them to get cards associated with the other crystals.

To win the game, two Tears of Power must be collected. One is obtained by accomplishing the quest (requires a deck optimisation); the other is obtained by successfully summoning Rhaluyne the Wise (requires enough crystals and a dice roll).

—description from the publisher (translated)
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In the near future, humanity suffers an existential threat in the form of a highly negative first contact with an alien race. The result is the Seeding – an alien invasion, but not a normal one. The invasion comes not in high-tech assault, but in a massive injection of foreign species, many only semi-intelligent.

Westhope centers around the actions of eight main characters who, through your actions, attempt to survive and resist.

Westhope is not simply a board game. It is a cluster of multiple games containing a common core mechanic called the Plan. For example, during a period the characters may be attempting to search for refugees over a wide area. They play a Journey mission. They search, evade or face enemies, working to survive while journeying over a large area. The next mission may be penetrating a facility, and they instead play a Tactical mission, where play style is more tactical RPG game with the Plan at its core. During Interludes, a specialized form of choose-your-own-adventure sets the characters and their allies on different paths, and pulls you into their lives so that they are far more than simply stats.

Westhope will have nearly every style of play available. Its core is an ambitious campaign where you truly begin to empathize with the characters and their allies (and enemies). However, players can engage in separate solo, cooperative, or competitive modes in each of the individual board game elements.

The campaign spans months and years, and expansions continue the story, some adding new board game methods to play.

—description from the publisher

Pfau ziehen is a simple tug-of-war game to lure peafowl using a sealed auction bid. 
The theme is based on a word play, because the German words for rope (Tau) and peafowl (Pfau) rhyme.

Players simultaniously play cards. The player with the highest card may pull the peafowl one step towards his goal. Tied players are ignored.
The first player to move the peafwol to his goal wins.

This appears to be one of several unlicensed themed versions of Monopoly Junior originating in China. Although Uncle Pennybags appears on the Monopoly Junior logo and (incongruously) on some game spaces and cards, there are no publisher logos or other crediting.

It seems there exist at least five cover variations: two with the Bakugan logo under the Monopoly logo ringed by characters, one of them with a flat Monopoly logo and one with a more elaborate fake-3D one; one with the Bakugan logo in the bottom left and three foreground characters; and two with the Bakugan logo at the bottom flanked by two foreground characters, one with English text and one with a Romanian flag and text.

The gameplay is identical to regular Monopoly Junior, with a nearly complete lack of player decisions. Players roll and move along the board, automatically purchasing visited properties, drawing chance cards, and performing monetary transactions until someone runs out of money. Properties are a selection of characters and other features from the Bakugan franchise.

Let Tank Girl and the gang help you as you try to collect as much gold as possible before time runs out!

The Tank Girl Super Game is a traditional board game in which Tank Girl and friends help you to collect as much gold as possible before time runs out. Includes an A4 card playing board, four coloured counters, thirteen illustrated game cards, cut-out Gold tokens, a mini die, and game instructions.

Genies is a bidding game about the world´s most famous scientists. Each player tries to hire scientists in his academy to score in three rankings in eight different disciplines. Therefore the players have to simultaneously make secret offers for five scientists each round. After each second round, there is a new ranking. Action cards can give the players special incentives concerning the profile of their academy. After the third ranking the player who has scored the most points in all rankings combined wins the game.

The players start at "Start". The first to reach the parking lot wins the game. Movement is determined by a die. At certain spaces, traffic related events hinder or help the players, as described in the game rules. The game is designed to teach children about traffic signs.

The older Liliput editions show the traffic signs from 1927. In 1934 the game was updated to the new traffic signs. Some of these are pictured with the old arrows still around them, being a combination of old and new.

The game is for 2 players or 2 teams of players. Movement is determined by 3 dice with the numbers 1 to 5. On 2 dice, the 6th side is blank (stud). On the 3rd die, the 6th side is red (called gas station attendant). Each player or team has 5 motorcycles and 5 numbered race tracks. If it's track number is rolled the motorcycle advances 1 space. If the player rolls red, he may move any motorcycle 4 spaces; if all 3 dice show the same number, the motorcycle on the track of this number is moved 4 spaces. The gas stations count for 4 spaces. Each motorcycle arriving at the finish line gets a set number of points, depending on the order of arrival. The player or team with the most points wins the game.

—user summary

The board has a square grid on the four sides, numbers 2 to 12. In the middle, 10 wooden trees are randomly placed and, at random, one pawn per player. Then each player rolls two dice, sums them up and places on one of the four boxed number one of the 10 wooden game animals. At this point you have to move the animal with the result of either of the two dice. With the other die you can do one of the following: cancel it and do nothing; move his pawn orthogonally, in the desired direction; move another animal already on the board, in the direction of the head. At the end of these movements, if the pawn of the turn player is orthogonally within 4 boxes from an animal (or even more than one) without the line of sight being obstructed, he can "snap a photo" by picking the corresponding card from the deck . The first one who manages to collect ten photos of the ten different animals won the game.

English board game from WWI.  No rules available or any information about how many players or how the players advance (dice or spinner).  Also the squares have different elements (solid red, red cross, and star) that have some kind of game play aspect.

Board says it is part of "Valentine's Series"

Odd game regarding the battle of Muckden. No rules or units exist as far as I know but the map speaks of reserve placement, cavalry, cossacks, railroads, cavalry charges and shots. Towns are noted by numbers and there are names of key General and towns.

This is a huge watercolor point to point board game of French battles in WWI that was drawn in 1915.  It does not have a title but the internet says its "La Guerre Moderne".  The internet also said it was published in 1914 but you can see on the bottom Série 1915 (Series 1915).  There appears to be an artist's name in the bottom left corner but it is not readable.

boardgame: Star Wars: The Card Game – Swayed by the Dark Side
description:
"You're Black Krrsantan. The infamous Wookiee bounty hunter. I've heard of you. I know why you were forced to leave Kashyyyk. And how you've disgraced your people ever since."
     —Obi-Wan Kenobi to Black Krrsantan

Swayed by the Dark Side is the fourth Force Pack in the Alliances cycle for Star Wars™: The Card Game. Its sixty new cards (divided between two copies each of five different objective sets), continue to explore the bonds of friendship, duty, and honor that drive many of the galaxy's pilots and soldiers, as well as the fear, hope, and greed that compel others to take action.

Among these new objective sets, you'll find new units and tactics for the Sith, Imperials, Scum, Jedi, and Rebels. You'll find cards to carry you from the Imperial strongholds of the Core Worlds to the forested moons of the Outer Rim. You'll find new enhancements, events, and Fate cards that support decks built around multiple affiliations. And you'll find a number of recognizable, high-impact characters taken straight from the films and Marvel comics, including Biggs Darklighter, the Ewok Logray, and the notorious Wookiee bounty hunter Black Krrsantan!

—description from the publisher

Contains objective sets 275-279
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Star Wars – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
NeoParadise: Rampage is a card game set in a science fiction, dark fantasy, post-apocalyptic world where 4 factions slaughter each other for "survival".

Main Features

     The 4 groups have different play styles but that's not all. Each faction either adds or changes a rule of the game to their advantage, but that's not all! Each faction also possess a different resource system!
     How to win, during the match players will play buildings (one of the 3 card types) to form their "base". This constructions can be damaged and the moment your enemy has no more of them in the field, you win.
     We have 2 ways of playing, one in which you play with your deck in 1 pile, and another in which you separate your deck in 2 piles. You can choose from which pile you draw your card each turn. This decreases luck and increases strategy.


Comments from the designer:

A big part of the game is that all factions are broken and fragile at the same time. All of them can win or lose against all of the others in a ridiculous way, specially when you get the hang of it.

In this game we have 3 card types, Units, Buildings and Interventions. Units represent your "troops", Buildings make your base and Interventions are standalone effects. You can't cancel enemy effects in this game, only normal attacks from 
Units.

While it's not difficult to learn, specially if you have played other card games, the fact that every faction is so different might make you feel you are learning 4 games at the same time...Might.

Also, when it comes to playing times, the one registered is the standard. But sometimes, specially when you really grasp the game and you get top level luck: you can get a 3 minute match... This happened only once during testing, quite a funny thing it was. And also, if you have no idea whatsoever and get terrible luck it can stretch up to an hour and a half. Only in those circunstances.

Battle of Bannockburn tries to simulate the battle that faced the English against the Scottish armies in the beginning of the 14th century (1314 A.C.). In this game you have to choose between english army or scottish army. The two players play face to face and both players have the same number of troops: 7 soldiers of infantry, 2 archers, 2 horse knight riders and the King. There are also, reserve forces. Each army has their own characteristics. In this case, the English have more soldiers (as in the real battle) but the Scottish infantry is stronger, so there is a balance. Careful, try not to lose the King. If your King dies you don't lose automatically the match,but you will lose some soldiers of infantry.
The aim of the game is to destroy the enemy army and win the battle for freedom.

—user summary

boardgame: Welt der Sinne: Hören, Sehen, Schmecken, Riechen, Fühlen
description:
"Sensory World – hearing, seeing, tasting, smelling, feeling"

Young Native Americans are preparing for their first sense-awareness trial. Sipokoo, the wise owl, guides the young Natives in mastering a wide variety of tasks, sharpening their senses. Where exactly is the bell? What figures can you identify with only your fingers? Which two bags are equally heavy? The game box includes everything you need for the basic games exploring hearing, seeing and feeling. Later on, household items can supplement the games related to smelling and tasting.

The basic game comprises 6 beginner’s trials for hearing, seeing and feeling.

Tasting and smelling games are added for advanced play variations.

Expert-level games challenge true nature scouts.

"Sensory World" contains overall 16 different games.

The natural forces in the South Pacific are out of alignment. The balance of power between the Tiki gods is in chaotic disorder. The Tiki families are at war with one another. Be the first player to collect a complete family (5 different Tikis of a single color) and bring peace and harmony back to the South Pacific.

You will have a three card hand. Each turn you will draw one card then play 1 card. The cards on the table in front of you are referred to as your tiki family.  At the beginning of the game there are no cards in front of you so your first 5 turns are spent building your family.  They do not need to match to have them in your tiki family. You just can't win until you have 5 different tikis with the same color.

boardgame: The Appalachian Trail Game: The ME-GA Expansion Set
description:
We are excited to announce NEW cards that expand the game play of both the Backpacking and the Special Edition of The Appalachian Trail Game. The ME-GA Expansion Set includes two distinct decks -- The Thru-Hiker Deck and the A.T. Communities Deck. These two decks combine for 25O new cards and mirror the dual backpacking experience of spending time in the backcountry and in A.T. Communities along the way.

To play with the ME-GA Expansion Set...


     Replace your old Challenge Cards with NEW Challenge cards from the ME-GA Expansion Set 
     Shuffle the NEW Chance cards in with your existing Chance cards 
     Get your GA-ME on!


—description from the publisher

A creative word and writing game inspired by Exquisite Corpse,  and somewhat similar to Eat Poop You Cat, but with creative writing as the underpinnings instead.

Each player writes a story fragment of some agreed-to size. They can either agree to a specific theme, voice, or tense, or they can have no such agreement at all. Then one player, known as the moderator, tells each player who to send his story fragment to. Each player will send his fragment to two different players, one with the subject "first" and the other with the subject "last". After a player receives a "first" and a "last" from other players, he must write a transition that connects the two story fragments together. The completed work is sent back to the moderator with the subject "transition", and the moderator puts together the complete story, linking fragment to transition to fragment to transition, and so on. The result is a story that loops, and is usually quite disconnected.

Belkin offers a diverse product mix that provides people with seamless electronics integration in their homes, cars, and on the go. Founded in California in 1983, Belkin is a privately held company and the recognized leader in connectivity solutions, in addition to accessories for MP3 devices, computers, and more. We provide extensive solutions for networking, home theater with our PureAV" line of cables, power, and digital wireless accessories and many more categories. You can view and purchase them at www.belkin.com, as well as through network of major distributors, resellers, and superstores.

Belkin's phenomenal growth has led to our expanding global presence, which is why we thought Belkin was a great fit for a Monopoly game. We hope your family and friends will enjoy buying, selling and trading Belkin locations worldwide while pursuing the dream of owning it all. What a better way to visit all of our offices than through the exciting game of Monopoly!

—description from the publisher

Go Huskies TM!

Monopoly, the legendary game of property and money, and the University of Washington, the celebrated university of academics and research, team up to create a classic board game for Husky M fans everywhere. Take a spin around the bdard to find the best loved buildings and scenes of one oRAntErRa's great universities. And be prepared to pay big bucks if you and Denny Hall or en Library the first building on campus and the campus' main library doesn't come cheap!

Grab your friends and family, put on your Husky M sweatshirt and enter the halls of UW for the game of your life. The Hub, Red Square, McMahon Hall, Burke Museum or 22 other UW properties can all be yours. Whether a student, alumnus or fan, can you resist the chance to buy Washington's finest?

GAME INCLUDES Game Board. Property Cards. Play Money and Banker's Tray. 2 Dice. 10 Game Tokens. Chance Cards. Community Chest. Cards. Package of Dormitories. Package of Houses.

Photography courtesy of University of Washington Athletics Media Relations and University Photography. Center Board Photo by Loyd C. Heath.
—description from the publisher

"Plan your combo. Save the city together. — A fun, cooperative, strategic crime-fighting card game."

Beatdown is a 1-4 player fully cooperative card game. Players team up to defeat waves of comic-book criminal and eventually go toe-to-toe with a crime-boss that threatens their city. The group wins if they are able to reach and defeat the boss and lose if all players are simultaneously reduced to 0 hitpoints.

The core fighting mechanic revolves around building combos by using a mixture of attack cards in hand and from the shared "Attack" deck. Between each attack players must roll a 10-sided combo die to see if they can continue their attack. Each added attack increases the difficulty rating of this roll. A failed combo roll leaves an opening for enemies to attack.

At the start of the game each player chooses a hero with a unique stat-line and ability. Heroes tend to have roles for group play such as tanky wrestlers or fragile high-damage boxers. Planning the right team to bring can be crucial for defeating the foes that await.
—description from the publisher
