Newsroom is a worker placement game where you get to manage your own international media outlet. The aim of the game is to publish your newspaper editions earlier than other players thus gaining the trust of your audience and of the advertisers and boosting your global presence as an International media outlet.

In the beginning of the round, news come out in different countries around the world in different fields (politics, economy, sports, culture). Each story is associated to countries with the roll of dice. Players have in their disposal a Press team and send its members to cover some of the stories, by interviewing people and getting their news copy back in the paper. 
 Each edition is divided in 4 blocks (newspaper columns) and has to cover at least 10 content points which derive from the news pieces that journalists bring back or, later in the game, from advertisements. Once the edition content is complete, the paper goes to the editors while journalists are free to get some more news stories from around the world.

After the editors have checked your story, the copy moves on to printing and is published. Its time to collect money, to pay your Press team and to hire better journalists, or more editors or printers to get your next edition even faster. Remember, the competition in the media is fierce and if you lose time the other media outlets will get your story out and it will then be worth nothing to you or your audience.

Based in the queuing theory, Newsroom is an easy to learn but hard to master worker placement- economic management game where speed and effectiveness determine the winner. Much like in the real media world!

—description from the designer

E-Penser : Le Jeu is a trivia game created by Bruce Benamran, a French YouTube personality known for his science channel E-Penser.

In this roll and move game, reminiscent of the Game of Goose, players have to reach the end spot while avoiding various traps and answering complex science trivia questions regarding space, physics, biology, human sciences and geek culture.

10 promotional cards from the associated Kickstarter campaign: https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/meromorphgames/norsaga-odds-and-endings-expansion

2 cards that expand Norsaga
Saga: Goddess of the Mists (Might, Faith, and so much Lore)
Saga: Goddess of the Mists (Might, Faith, and so much Craft)

2 cards that expand Norsaga: Into the Myths

Ghost Legend: Yldivard Ash-Coat

6 cards that expand/combine Norsaga: Into the Myths and Norsaga: Odds and Endings
Heirloom - The Empty Mask: Fimble
Immortal - Guardian: Thrice-Woke Omhar
Immortal - Guardian: Ulwilda Fading
Immortal - Guardian: Agmind the Intrepid
Immortal - Guardian: Soul-catcher
Immortal - Watcher: Unsung Donwen

Gameplay
The 2 Saga cards function identically to other Saga cards.

The new Ghost Legend and Fae Legend hybrids combine traits from various hero cards seen in Norsaga: Into the Myths.
The Ghost Legend obeys all rules for both Ghosts and Legends. In your hand it is considered to be a card with black, white, green, red, yellow, and blue colors. It can be played onto any family tree, and must immediately receive an inheritance marker after being played. Until it leaves play it counts as a black card and a white card in addition to the color of that inheritance marker. The inheritance marker may not be removed until the Ghost Legend is dismissed.

The Fae Legend behaves similarly to the Ghost Legend, except for the differences between Ghost and Fae cards. When the Fae Legend is played onto a player's family tree, that player must then swap it per normal Fae rules.

The new Wanderer Guardians and Fae Watcher can be shuffled into the deck of Immortals to provide additional variety.
The Wanderer Guardians have more flexible unlock tracks, allowing you to unlock them in either 2 or 3 steps. While on top of a hero on your tree, the Guardian's traits are used.

The Fae Heirloom can be shuffled into the deck of Heirlooms to provide additional variety.
Do not use this card if you are not playing with Fae and Skalds. Once the Heirloom is restored, your youngest Hero gains the Fae's Lost trait. This does not cause any additional swapping to occur when playing to that spot on your family tree.

The Skald Watcher has a flexible unlock track that requires you to have a Ghost or Fae on your tree, followed by a Skald. Do not use this card if you are not playing with Ghosts/Fae and Skalds. Once unlocked, the Skald Watcher allows you to use any of the four basic 1st-generation embellishments.

- Magic Item: Leather Armor +2
- Encounter: Manticore

Ascension: Year Four Collector's Edition includes Ascension: Realms Unraveled and Ascension: Dawn of Champions, as well as all two copies of the promo cards from that year. The set also includes 100% foil cards, foil game board, portable storage box, dividers, and an Honor token bag!

A game for two players by Alain Couchot and Bernard Klein

Winner of the Golden Pawn at the 1997 Boulogne Billancourt Game Design Competition (under the name Tor)

COMPONENTS

An 8 × 8 gameboard of 64 neutral spaces.
4 rings (2 white and 2 black).
64 color discs (16 blue, 16 purple, 16 orange, 16 yellow).

OVERVIEW

Each player has two rings to move on the board. At the beginning, each space of the board is one of the 4 colors (blue, purple, orange, or yellow), and the colors are distributed evenly on the board. As the game progresses, space colors change, and certain spaces become inaccessible.

If you can move neither of your rings at the beginning of your turn, you lose, and your opponent wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Congo: Mungo Mah Lobeh – The Throne of Thunder
description:
Congo Mary: The Throne of Thunder is a complete campaign for Congo, Adventures in the Heart of Africa.  Congo Mary recounts one of the heroic journeys of British explorer Mary Kingsley.  It culminates with her ascension of Mount Camaroon - also known as the Throne of Thunder.

The campaign is made up of a series of adventures, linked together by an ongoing story-line and by special rules that allow players to develop and hopefully flourish as the plot unfolds.  This campaign will entertain and surprise you, offer many hours of wonder, some stimulating setbacks and considerable pleasure.

This campaign has been written specifically for two players, each one stepping into the shoes of one of the two protagonists of this perilous quest:  British explorer Mary Kingsley  and Uguwa a powerful sorcerer.

After facing up to the challenge of six new adventures (six new scenarios) you will face off in what we guarantee to be an epic and breathtaking finale.

boardgame: Legends of the Ancient World: Punisher's Keep
description:

Unfortunately, the relative is held at Punisher's Keep, the local Lord's prison, where the worst criminals in the land are held, and tortured. Some say that the Chief Inquisitor also conducts cruel experiments on the victims, but that may just be rumor...

Punisher'S Keep continues the series of paragraph driven adventure games from Dark City Games. Playable as a standalone dungeon crawl game, or compatible with The Fantasy Trip, GURPS, and Melee/Wizard.

description:
All things being equal the next election could go either way. It's up to you to make sure it swings in your party's favor. Campaign to encourage your voters and discourage your opponent's, change voter trends, and manipulate political boundaries to make sure your party gets into office. Players rearrange, flip, and replace tiles while manipulating the borders that make up the districts in game about American politics.

—description from the publisher

Lost Atlantis is described by the publisher as a "3X game under the sea".

Lost Atlantis is now an underwater exploration game where players gather clues to discover sunken pyramids, defeat their guardians, and collect treasures. The core of the game is a puzzle revealed by compass directions found by players on seafloor tiles. Players use these directions to scour the quadrants of the board for clues to discover pyramids. There is a central portal in the middle of the board that accumulates player scoring tokens as pyramids are claimed, and the game ends when all the central portal spaces are filled up. The player who accumulates the most Victory points throughout the game wins.

Red Sonja: Hyrkania's Legacy is a cooperative, modular-adventure board game for 1-4 players, expandable to 6.

The game allows players to participate as one of their favourite Red Sonja Comic Book characters ranging from the Barbarian Osin, the famous Red Sonja herself. These heroes will champion all that is the Hyborian good in 90 minutes as the players travel across the realm of Hyrkania and face dangerous warriors, mythical beasts and the walking souls that haunt the lands.

Collect lore-enriched relics, artifacts, weapons and special awards under a heated deadline while re-telling some of Red Sonja's best adventures in comic book and open source fictions history!

This game will challenge you and excite you. Everything you need is provided at the gaming table, just bring your friends, your love of Red Sonja (knowledge of Red Sonja lore optional), and your thirst for adventure! Onward to a world of high adventure!

—description from the publisher

The overpopulated Earth is slowly dying. Three human Factions: The Confederation of Sol, the Outer-Worlds Alliance and the Praetorans of Mars have been fighting for domination of the solar system with space fleets from their bases orbiting Earth, Mars and Titan.

Now with the perfection of the FTL hyper drive, Scout-ships are finally able to explore distant stars. Earth, Mars and Titan have agreed a truce in order to settle colonists in new star systems far from our star.

With a small fleet of ships, you must seek out and settling habitable worlds, but you would be wise to hire warships from these factions to protect your Outposts, for the future of these colonies will be beyond the control of Earth.

Send out your Scouts to explore new systems, send out your Transports to bring back rare minerals and gasses to finance your fleet. Hire warships from the Confederation, Alliance and Praetoran navies for your private fleet.

Build Outpost ships and finally Colony ships to settle worlds and claim the title of Star Lord!

In the game you will upgrade your starting deck of Scout ships and Basic Transports with a fleet of warship cards from the three space faring factions. You can buy Outpost ships to build Outposts and later upgrade these to Colonies on suitable worlds you have explored or else capture them from other players. You may control 4 systems at any time by deploying your ships there.

Objective: Control 4 Victory stars. Each Outpost is 1 star, Each Colony (upgrading Outpost) worth 2 stars. This means you win immediately when you control a Colony on 2 worlds, or an Outpost on 4 worlds.

The main USPs for Star Colonies are:  

     Area control (of star systems). Deploying ships to defend or to trade: carrying over unused credits, but you take a risk: extra Credits or nothing if system is seized.  
     Flip discard to draw deck (not shuffle!) so build order stays intact. In the advanced game we add Infiltrators as deck manipulators.  
     Interaction: ability to intercept an attack (with cards from hand) or to target specific star systems and deployed enemy ships.  


The full game works just as well with 2, 3 or 4 players. Players can start with a more peaceful tutor-mode game before the full on strategy game with the full card set.

—description from the designer

boardgame: バイバイレミング (Bye-Bye LEMMING)
description:
The Lemmings were getting too comfortable where they lived.
There were too many Lemmings because they have been eating too much.
Now, there was no food left so the Lemmings decided to leave and try and find a new place to live.

Timing is important especially when starting out on a journey. When one Lemming starts out other Lemmings will follow, because they have the same instincts. The object of the game is to be the first to send out all your Lemmings.

—description from the publisher

Turning Tides is a tile placement game. Play time can range from 30 to 45 minutes depending on skill level.

Players place tide pools, each containing a critter, and seek to create roads to the tide pool by matching compatible edges. Some tide pools will disappear faster than others as rising waters threaten shallower pools. As players build out a unique set of tide pools, they may also block off any player who seeks to create a continuous path.

—description from the designer

Adorable Monsters is the super cute card game, for all ages, in which the first player to put together a squad of 7 cutie patootie monsters, wins! Have a blast with your buds, pals, mates, friends, family, or even absolute strangers, as you try to be the first to create your monster squad!

How to Play
Learning to play Adorable Monsters is easy! That's because what each card can be used to do during play is written directly on it, in the form of concise instructions.

To begin the game, all players draw 5 cards and thereafter take turns drawing and playing one card at a time, as they try to line up a squad of 7 monsters in front of them to win the game.

Of course, it's not that easy, as there are lots of cards that players can use to remove monsters from their opponents' squads. So, not only do you have to try to put together your monster squad, but you have to try to stop your friends from doing so, too!

—description from the publisher

With a unique cast of characters with special powers from the Eisner Award-nominated comic series, you and up to seven friends play as a gang of shifty shady felines trying to keep their speakeasy afloat.

The game is cooperative with all players loading into their car, the Fordor, and exploring a randomly arranged deck of location cards laid out face down on the table. Players must get enough of "The Good Stuff" back to the Lackadaisy Speakeasy before the time runs out and the speakeasy folds like a cheap suit. Players will need to arm themselves and use the special location powers to their best ability to win. With event cards that can help as much as harm, the tide of the game can turn at any moment.

boardgame: Roads and Rails, Rivers and Trails
description: Play your cards to build a shared network of roads, rails, rivers, and trails. Block your opponents while scoring paths for yourself -- but don't forget about any of your own paths, or you'll be caught short at the end!

Owen's Chase is an asymmetrical 2-player deduction game set in the 19th century Nantucket. Whales are trying to safely travel north to the feeding grounds to feast on rich krill while being pursued by a sharp lookout in the crow's nest of a whaling ship. On each expedition, use your senses to track down a whale in the vast ocean. But be careful of dangerous weather, unexpected repairs, or even a nasty tail fluke that can bring your chase quickly to an end.

One player plays as the Whale and the other as the Lookout. The Whale player puts the cards face down in a 4 x 4 grid, and the Lookout chooses cards. Based on the card chosen when revealed, various actions occur that can have the Lookout win, the Whale win, or other results. Play continues until the Whale is found or or the Shipyard is found, or until the expedition ends. Cards include the Whale, a tail attack, crow's nest, open ocean, shipyard, and storm.

—user summary

The war had raged for millennia. Or it started just a week ago, depending on how you look at it. Time travel can be quite confusing. What's known for certain is that the fighting is between four different sides: The Aliens, the Apes and Monkeys, the Robots, and the Space Vikings. They all got access to the temporal technologies at the same time, not that "same time" means much in this situation. With time travel it doesn't really matter *when* it's invented...

Each player is sending out their agents to different points in the timestream, trying to control key time periods and technologies. By planning your moves right you can re-use the same cards multiple times, and even revive agents killed in action!

Time War(p) is a quick card game for two to four players ages 13 and up.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: World of Warcraft Trading Card Game: Drums of War
description:
The time has come to prepare for battle, as Upper Deck Entertainment Introduces World of Warcraft "Drums of War- PVP Battle Decks

The thundering drums of war echo throughout the land of Kalimdor. New warlords ride through the gates of Orgrimmar, eager to serve the warchief and leave their marks on the pages of history.
Small battalions of Alliance soldiers arrive at outposts throughout Azeroth.
They solidify their footholds in contested areas as marshals draw up strategies to defend their territories and press any aggressors who foolishly cross their paths.
It is only a matter of time until the piercing battle cries, roaring magic, and crashing steel evoke the symphony of war once again.

When the dust settles, will you be remembered, or will you fade into oblivion?

Call to Arms!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: A'Writhe: A Game of Eldritch Contortions
description:
Summon the Great Old Ones by having them align their bodies over Arkham.

In the game A'Writhe: A Game of Eldritch Contortions, players gather in teams of two. Each team consists of a cultist player and a Great Old One player. The cultist is assisting the Great Old One and attempting to summon them to our plane. To do this, the cultist instructs their deity, with great veneration, to place an appendage on top of an Arkham landmark to complete a specific pattern. The problem is that it is nigh impossible to have one Great Old One form this pattern by itself; that's why if another deity is touching any parts of your pattern, you can use that appendage to complete your own pattern!

Up to three teams of two can play in this contortionist battle of positioning.

—description from the publisher

A deck of 108 "Tactical Playing Cards" (or T-cards) to use when playing an ASL scenario.

Each player receives at game start a number of cards from the deck and may play them whenever he sees fit during a scenario. Each card has an effect printed on it, just apply what is written on the card and discard the card. The total number of cards in the deck is sufficiently large to ensure that no hand will be similar to another. The rules underpinning T-cards' use are as concise as possible.

Travel around the most beautiful places in XVIth century Europe with Montaigne!
In 1580 and 1581, Michel de Montaigne is travelling Europe on horseback. In a Travel journal, he writes of this extraordinary experience.
Become travelers and follow his steps around France, Germany, Switzerland or Italy, to discover countries through Montaigne’s eyes, meet people he encountered and think with his mind… Until King Henri III calls you back to Bordeaux, as he did with Montaigne asking him to become the city’s mayor, then you will have to end your journey and go back to the South West of France as fast as you can.

This is a multi-purpose card game for 2 to 4 players from 8 years old.
The players manage their best hand cards they will pose in front of them, deploying the many discoveries of their trip (gastronomy, meetings, visits or reading the Montaigne books ...), while observing the choices of other players.
Voyages avec Montaigne offers many mechanics interlaced in a simple and fluid way. Opportunism, interaction and anticipation make the pleasure of a game that suddenly makes one of the greatest French writers of all time very close.

In the game Voyages avec Montaigne, players place cards face up next to the last card they placed, on a line from left to right. This line represents their journey.

On their turn, players take turns with 1 action among 3 : 

     Travel : place a card from a different city AND pay the cost of the travel (0, 1 or 2 cards discarded)
     Stay : placing a card of the same city AND pick 1 new card
     Get supplies : not placing a card AND pick  and 2 new cards


Players can discard a figure met during his journey to take another action OR take a card from the discard pile.

2 travelling rules must be respected :

     At the end of their journey, players must return to Bordeaux and have placed at least on « Citation » card form each of the 3 books of Montaigne
     Players cannot go back to a city they have already been.


During their journey, players take one of the Bonus tile available as soon as they reach the objective first or do better than a player who had already achieved the objective (5 discovery bonus, 4 book bonus).

When the « King’s call » special card is revealed, all players must go back to Bordeaux as soon as possible, rules are thus modified :

     Action « Stay » is not allowed anymore
     Players cannot travel to a city that would take them further away from Bordeaux.


First players returning to Bordeaux get bonus, As soon as all players but one have returned to Bordeaux, the last player only has one turn left to play.

Bonus tiles are awarded to players : most cities visited, the most different theme cards, the most different characters encountered, largest stay.

The player with the highest score wins.

Players try to win elections by playing cards. At the beginning, two dice are thrown and multiplied by ten thousand to know how many votes must be obtained to win.

Players then place positive cards in front, and opponents can place negative cards in front of others. Each of the five different positive cards allows you to start a stack.

A player can not have more than one stack of the same quality.

If you are blocked in a stack, you can unlock it with a positive card of the same type.

According to the stacks that you have unlocked you can place votes in front of you. These do not count how one of the stacks allowed.

There are televised debates that begin with a card, and that are resolved by rolling a dice each opponent and adding their unblocked stacks.

Win the first to get the necessary votes.

—user summary

Rising Rage adds:
5 New Dice
4 New Weapons (8 cards total)
20 New Techniques (+ 3 additional Basic School cards)

As you might imagine, the expansion focuses on the rage mechanic in the game, giving players more ways to use and acquire rage.

The new cards often feature ways for you to acquire or spend Rage Tokens, and the new dice feature a Rage Result allowing players a way to collect Rage Tokens without changing their guard!

The addition of the expansion tweaks the rules in small ways (Weapons for example are chosen from a random selection instead of the complete arsenal of 8+ weapon Cards) but otherwise simply adds more ways for you to show off your martial mastery and manipulate the power you put into each attack! The Expansion also works with the 4-player rules.

What: Wargame/RPG rules for World War II. 
Scale: Man to man combat. 1 figure equals 1 man; 1 model equals 1 vehicle.
Your Role: Players can command from one squad to a platoon of infantry or from one platoon to a company of tanks.
Playability: Designed for solo, same side and head to head play.

NUTS can be played in a variety of ways:

You can play as a Squad Leader with a full squad.
You can play as a Squad Leader with less than a full squad.
You can play as a Fighting Vehicle Platoon Leader with three to five vehicles.
You can play with only one Fighting Vehicle.
You can play large games with multiple squads and vehicles.

The bottom line is you can play it any way you like, with any figures or counters you like.

Inside you’ll find:


     Rules for infantry combat with over 15 different formations.

     Thirty-five unique attributes to personalize your squad members.

     A minimal bookkeeping Campaign System that gives meaning to every game as the results of one will affect the outcome of the next.

You are invited to a Killer Dinner! However, this is no ordinary dinner party. Everyone is masked and has their own hidden agenda. Who is your friend, and who is planning on stabbing you tonight?
Ask questions, collect items, and attack other partygoers to accomplish your hidden agenda before anyone else. Blend in while figuring out your fellow partygoers’ hidden agendas before it’s too late!

At the beginning of the game each player gets a card with their secret role and hidden agenda. Attending the party are The Host, The BFF, The Trickster, and The Hitman. Everyone, except the Hitman, is also informed of the Host’s profession on their Role Card. 
The Host’s agenda is to figure out who the Hitman is and to kill them. 
The BFF’s agenda is to help and protect the Host. 
The Trickster’s agenda is to attack or get attacked a total of seven times. 
The Hitman’s agenda is to figure out the Host’s profession, or to kill them.
While in the same room, players may ask each other yes/no questions to discern information about the Host’s profession, revealing who the Hitman is based on incorrect answers. Players explore the house and collect items upon entering an empty room. Items can be used to help gather information, attack other players, or replenish lost Health.
Whoever accomplishes their hidden agenda first, wins the game.

Killer Dinner is a party game that will create a new social dynamic each time it’s played. A great number of Host’s professions and the random role allocation, makes every game different. Due to the social aspect and asking questions the game is a good opportunity to get to know your fellows players better!

Prime Number Five is very easy mathmatic memory game.
Contain 30 cards.
All cards has frontside number and backside number.
Each card, frontside and backside has a some relation.

Backside numbers are 2, 3, 5, 7, and 11. (5 types number x 6 cards)
Frontside numbers are 4, 6, 9, 10, 14, 15, 21, 22, 25, 33, 35, 49, 55, 77, and 121. (15 types number x 2 cards)

If frontside number is 14, then the card's backside is 2 or 7.
Because 14=2x7
If backside number is 3, then the card's frontside is 6, 9, 15, 21, or 33.

Shuffle all cards, and spread them on table.
In your turn, reveal one card and another.
If the two cards's frontside numbers are same, you get it.
Get, or not get, next player's turn.

—description from the publisher

The game consists of a board based on a railway map of Switzerland, and around 100 cardboard tokens labeled with the names of each station.

The object is to be the first player to complete a pre-agreed journey across the map by rolling a die and moving. A six gets another turn but if a second six is rolled then the player must move backwards. Mountain and local lines can only be traversed when the correct number is thrown.

The rules sheet (bilingual German/French) give 10 different variants for how the players' routes may be chosen: in some the tokens are placed on the map and collected, in others the tokens are used to draw a station at random.

Publisher: Papa Schubi № 2822.

The throne has remained vacant for far too long, demons assault the city, and the people have become desperate. You have travelled far to arrive in the city of Bloodstone. The desperate situation in the city has forced the Council to reach out to the powerful Demigods in hopes of finding a new ruler. You have five days to gain the favour of the council and the people. They are eager to be impressed.
    Your paths to victory are numerous: complete quests to gain favor and influence; slay the demons that spawn each night and harvest them for the riches that lay within; amass great wealth and purchase symbols of status to impress the masses. 
        You are not the only hero to see this as their opportunity. Others have journeyed to the city and will fight to impede your progress at every turn. Can you win the favour of the Council and become the next Ruler?

Demons and Demigods is a deep strategy game with immense replayability. In fact, no two games will ever be the same.

Players can all play with the same faction abilities by playing as "Civilians", or each player can choose to play as a Demigod - a unique faction with special abilities and Rages. Play as a Warlock, Earth Elemental, Giant, or one of the other unique Demigods.

Throughout 5 days, players will track their time using a unique and intuitive time track. This track consists of 24 spaces. Each player begins with 12 time before the Midnight Oil ends their day, but through upgrades it is possible to improve Ambition and start the day with more time. Each movement through the city and actions at a building costs time, so plan your route and day wisely. Whoever has the most time remaining in the day will always play next - so player order is dynamic. Once you enter the Midnight Oil your day will end.

Players move their characters throughout the game board using a one-of-a-kind worker placement mechanic. Your character blocks building action spaces. When you move, the building action space becomes available. Each game includes 9 randomly placed buildings. Depending on your strategy and immediate needs you may want to visit the Butcher to gain Bloodstones, or the Blacksmith to buy gear. You may wish to travel to the Market to make some important trades, or maybe you need to visit the Grand Palace to improve your influence. During all of this, your character is also tasked with slaying the demons that lurk at buildings and playing quests. Slaying demons and playing quests allow a character to gain influence at a building. This is tracked through a dynamic area control mechanic which makes use of a character's energy. Quests are also assigned to 1 of the 5 council members. Each member has specific themes attached to the quest, and at the end of the game, you are rewarded for having a variety of council members showing. This means you must diversify your strategies in order to impress the entire council.

Each day players will try to improve their engine to maximize the income at Day's End. They do this by unlocking dormant cubes and visiting the Philosopher's Study for upgrades. Spend the Bloodstones you have collected from slaying demons to upgrade your character. Some upgrade examples are: Income (silver, gold, energy regeneration, etc.), and Ambition (more time).

Your character starts with 18 energy cubes. These cubes are incredibly dynamic and will be used for combat with demons, upgrading abilities, area control influence, and more. How you manage your energy will greatly affect your chances of success.

At the end of the game players will score points based on their demon slays, influence, council support, and resources.

Can you be the next Ruler of Bloodstone?

This is the 7th in a series of Annual Games Unlimited Holiday Games and is the followup to Gameshop Express (2011), Gameshop Workday (2012), Gameshop Gladiators (2013), Gameshop Adventurers (2014), Gameshop Expansions (2015), and Gameshop Collector (2016).

The Mirror Universe employees of Unlimited Games have come to our reality to battle gurus Kylie, Candra and Andrew for control over the Games Unlimited store. Only one game team will reign supreme!

To Win: Place all your active Guru markers (any remaining on the board) on any of the gray marked spaces in your opponent’s home row or remove all of your opponent’s markers.

Setup: Place the board between players and orient it so that one player is facing the 3 Games Unlimited Gurus and the other player their mirror duplicate Gurus from Unlimited Games. The row closest to the player is their home row. Each player receives 
3 Guru markers in a matching color. Place these markers, 
1 each, on the 3 gray spaces on your home row. Shuffle the 
4 Action cards and, without looking at them, flip half the cards over to their opposite side. Deal 1 card face up in front of each player and place 1 card to the side of the board. The 4th card is removed from the game. The first player will be the one who has most recently looked in a mirror.

Play: Each player has an Action card that displays a possible move for any of their markers. There is a 3rd card to one side of the board that cannot be used by either player during their turn. On a player’s turn, they move 1 of their active Guru markers (Note: Active Guru markers are the ones remaining on the board) according to the Action card; they then replace the card they used with the 3rd card. The other player then moves accordingly, and exchanges their card with this new 3rd card - the card the first player just used.
Moving a marker on top of one of an opponent player’s markers removes that opponent’s marker from the game.

A player may stack a maximum of 2 of their pieces on any space except the center space. A stack prevents either stacked piece from being captured.

Moving your active Guru markers to the home row spaces of 
your opponent wins you the game.

As long as one of a player’s markers remains on the center space on the board, that player may, during their turn, flip their Action card over to its opposite face to introduce a new move into the game.

Action cards should be oriented with the name of the action at the bottom of the card. Each card shows the player’s marker in the middle of the card and indicates which spaces, either forward, backward or to the side, to which they may move their marker. Below this diagram, the action on the other face of the card is displayed.

—description from the publisher

description:
Kingdom 18 is an 18 card hand management game with hints of deck building. Players take the role of nobles attempting to build kingdoms either through diplomacy or force. The goal is to grab the highest point value lands and create the most powerful kingdom or take less valuable lands that offer you powerful actions later.

Players start with a hand of 4 cards, each with 2 possible actions. These can be different values of military might or political savvy, or they can be an assassin or a shield that can steal the values of other cards their opponents play.

Each turn players place 1 card facedown on each land. These cards are revealed and another turn commences. After the second turn the players with the most strength on the land wins it. Bonus points are awarded for matching the types of cards the land prefers.

Collected lands count towards the end of the game scoring but also have their own in-game action. They are shuffled into the player's starting 4 cards and 4 new cards are drawn. The lands can have strong or weak abilities but only have one option.

Once all lands have been claimed the final scores are tallied and the highest score is the winner.

—description from the publisher

Itty Bitty Dungeon Delve is an 18 card tableau manipulation for 2 players. Each player represents a new adventurer exploring the cavernous rooms of a dungeon. Each is looking to gain experience and reach level 5 first, and they do so by rearranging the cards representing each individual cavern in the dungeon.

Players reveal cards from their cavern to rearrange facedown cards or gain knowledge of what the cards may be. At the end of the round they gain experience equal points from their cavern. If a player ends the turn with "The Smiter" card, they may instead choose another cavern to explore.

The first player to reach Level 5 wins!

—description from the designer

Sengoku: The Warring States is an 18 card combat game. Players take the role of shoguns attempting to take out a rival with the use of their small army.

Over 3 phases players deploy 6 units to attack their opponents villages with the goal of destroying two villages, or the village containing their opponent's shogun. Doing so wins the player a battle. Soldiers remain facedown until the end of phase 3 or when an opposing soldier is placed down as well.

The first player to win two battles is victorious!

18 Poker Cards
Folding Box/Rules

description:
This new version of Atlantikwall has been redesigned and vastly expanded.

In addition to its 6 Battle Maps, including Omaha Beach II & Exercise: Fabius, Atlantikwall contains 3 “mini-expansions” that are completely independent from each other. Players can add 1, or 2, or all of them to their game:

Atlantikwall: This module lets one player play the German side on any existing Battle Map. The German player can transform a 2-player game into a head-to-head confrontation, or be added to any group of players (even solo). Includes a full set of unique German Specialists, German Items and Decorations, a set of Red, White & Black dice, a player aid, a Resource card, and some additional Allied Specialists and Items.

Think of the German player as the Battle Map itself. It is up to him or her to “activate” some of the features of the Map, and to make life difficult for the Allies. He plays like all the other players, rolling dice to gather resources, and spending those resources to recruit Specialists and find Items. The main difference is that the German does not move: instead, he uses his Courage to draw cards from the "Decorations" deck, which give him special one-time abilities.

Battle Conditions: This deck of 50 cards reflects the unpredictability of battlefield conditions, whether it’s from nature itself or the hazards of combat. Drawn at the start of each turn, they affect all players and supersede Sector attributes, offering a variety of effects like friendly fire or smoke cover. Some are good, some are bad, and some offer opportunities to be seized at the right moment. Beware of plane crashes, supply trucks catching fire or loss of morale!

Badges: A reprint of the 1st edition hard-to-find mini-expansion, consisting of 10 cards. Badges can be drawn at random by a Unit that obtains a "Battle Cry" RWB. They have a one-time effect (much like Items), usually helping their Unit obtain RWB bonuses or Awards more easily.

This is a pair of two maps :

     In the Democratic Republic of Congo, more than half of the gross domestic product is derived from the agricultural sector, and fire is a major component, as it is in most of Africa. Fire is often used to burn forests and other natural vegetation to clear the way for new farmlands.


In this scenario, the fire has gotten terribly out of hand, slowly enveloping the country in flames. A very challenging scenario for any railway tycoon attempting to fire up business there.

Fugo is a territory game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square grid (board).

				
				
					DefinitionsA group is a stone along with all stones one can reach from it through a series of steps onto orthogonally adjacent stones of its color.

A liberty of a group is an empty point orthogonally adjacent to it.

A smothered group is a group without liberties.

				
				
					PlayBlack plays first, then turns alternate. At the start of your turn, you will face one of these situations:


     There are no smothered friendly groups. In this case, you must place a stone of your color on an empty point. Your placement must not increase the number of smothered friendly groups unless it also increases the number of smothered enemy groups.



     There are one or more smothered friendly groups. In this case, you must move one stone from one of those groups to an empty point such that, after the move, the group that now includes the moved stone has at least one liberty. You may only move a stone from a point where it is orthogonally adjacent to one or more enemy stones.


If you have no moves available on your turn, you lose. Draws are not possible.

To make the game fair, White will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Black instead of making a regular move.

description: This is a set of four cards to add Norman Withers to Arkham Horror: The Card Game. They were included with copies of the novella Ire of the Void.
family: Game: Arkham Horror – The Card Game
Game: Arkham Horror TCG – Books & Replacement Cards
Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
Add 30 all new event cards to your games! Keep your squad guessing with these dangerous and delightful arcane happenings. Compatible with all sets and add-ons!

—description from the publisher

Play an all new multi-mission Operation!

Forward observer base Gecko near Tobruk is reporting sightings of watchers just outside their perimeter. Army intelligence is discounting the reports due to the insistence of the men that the watchers are "devils" and chalking the reports up to battle stress.

Despite being unable to match the descriptions to known entities, Division Zero HQ is convinced that something is very wrong at Gecko. Survive 3 new Acts in Operation Harbinger and discover the fate of Forward Base Gecko.

—description from the publisher

BuzzPutt is a 1-4 player golf game that has you playing adorable bees! There are three pre-made putt putt courses that you and your friends can play on. Each person will select a bee and its matching ball. You have to place the ball on the starting point of the board, with the arrow pointing towards where you want to move.

Then, you roll the dice and pick a shot (basically a stroke in golf) based on what you have rolled. You can now move your ball that far. If you land on certain tiles, such as the honeycomb tiles, it'll cost you more movement points to move out. If you end up out of bounds, you must end your turn and start your next turn on the last tile you touched. Once everyone is at the end, you can move onto the next course.

—description from the designer

Pusher Kings is a funny, two-player card game that has you struggling to maintain control of the drug market in your town! This game is not a deck builder, so both players will be playing from a pre-set deck. Each player gets a hand of seven cards (with at least one "pusher" card in their hand) before the game starts. You must flip cards to use them, only using specific cards for your turn.

There are a few different card types; Pushers, Henchmen, and Actions. Pushers sell the drugs and can be used once per turn. Once you flip the card over in order to use them you gain money that can only be spent during that turn. Once the turn is over, the money disappears. Pushers cannot attack, defend, or take damage and only one can be used per turn.

Henchman have different costs, abilities, and attributes. They are used to gain power in the town and can be attacked. Henchman also cost a fair amount of money each, so you will need to be able to pay them to use them in your turn. The first turn you draw a henchman, you have to have them flipped, so they cannot be played right away.

Action cards are not flipped but instead discarded. There are only two of each action card in the deck, so you need to play them wisely. Each action is immediate and costs resources to play. These can be used to harm player’s henchmen or the player themselves.

Each player has 10 health points (the player themselves who holds the cards) so once they have been harmed enough to reach zero health points, the game is over.

Pusher Kings has some really awesome artwork drawn on the cards! This card game does not have deck building aspects, so it is an easy game to pick up and play with your friends.

—description from the designer

House Rules is a print and play party game where you make the rules!Take turns racing around the board, drawing cards with rules on them and collecting Star Tokens. One turn you might get a Board Rule that advances you three spaces. On your next you might get a Player Rule telling you to:


        Win a 10 second round of Charades or return to your last space
        Tweet to your secret crush or lose a Star Token
        Sketch a player in under 30 seconds AND get their approval
        Break into teams of two, have one player pick a secret word and get the other to answer it - the first team to guess their word wins Star Tokens


Everything you need to play is included in the download. Just print and play!

—description from the designer

Freight Wars is a 2-3 player game where you can procedurally generate your game tiles before you print them, giving you a completely unique game set. You are a Victorian industrialist who is looking to build a transportation network. You can do this by placing roads, rivers, or railways to connect towns to other places, as well as block off the routes of your opponents.

First, you must choose which mode of transportation each player should use. One should use roads, one rivers, the other should use trains. Then, you must shuffle the cards and hand them out to various players. Each player should get a town card, as well as two other cards dealt to them.

Players must place tiles so that the modes of transportation connect to the tiles already placed on the table. These tiles will need to match up for every mode of transportation to be placed, then the player will be able to draw another tile from the deck, so they always have three cards. Players can also skip a turn by discarding all of their tiles to draw a new hand.

After all of the tiles are placed, players must count up how many connections they have between towns. There are different points depending on how well the transport links are between different places. This can be seen in more detail in the rules of the game.
—description from the designer

This print-and-play game has an unusual distribution method - players download a Windows executable program which generates random tiles for printing.  Once printed, the tiles are used for traditional tabletop play.  Because the tiles are programmatically generated with randomization, no two sets will be exactly the same.

—user summary

Friends gather to grill and eat massive quantities of meat at a backyard barbecue, but there’s not enough space on the grill!

This card game features simultaneous action, variable player powers, hand-management, take-that and a towel to avoid the dreaded Meat Sweats.

The first player to eat 7 pieces of meat ends the game, but the player that consumed the highest combination of quantity and quality meat wins (most points).

Each round, grill cards are spread out in the center of the table to represent meat on the grill. These cards also have a hidden value on the back of the card based on the quality of the meat (for end game scoring).

Players simultaneously position their characters next to a grill card that they will attempt to eat.  Players then simultaneously play a meat craving card (face down) from their hand and reveal.  If players have a matching meat they can score it, unless someone else wants the same meat and is willing to pay more matching cards for it. No one scores ties, unless you or a temporary ally play a Meat Sweats card on your opponent. If you receive the meat sweats card, you're unable to claim meat this round, but you get to keep the meat sweats card, making you more powerful later on!

—description from the publisher

A simple cardgame based on a Dutch comedy series.

Players have to match numbers or colors to the discard pile. The first to have an empty hand wins the game. Some cards instruct players to take more cards or skip turns.

—user summary

名謎偵探 (Mystery Detective) is a fast-paced competitive logical deduction game for 3-8 players.  A suspect, a motive, a weapon, and a location are chosen at random from a deck of cards, then players take turns interrogating each other for information about each of the aspects of the crime (by taking a card of the chosen type from another detective).  When one player thinks they have the solution, they compare their answer with the hidden cards.  If they are correct, they win!  However, if any part of their answer is incorrect, the game continues and the foolish detective is eliminated.

Pocket Skirmish is a fast-paced tactical card game for 2 players, in which you assume control over 3 units on a randomly generated map. With tactics and luck, you achieve victory by defeating all of your opponent's units!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Test of Honour: The Samurai Miniatures Game – Ninjas of Iga
description:
Hiding in the shadows, creeping in the corner of your eyes, the Ninja of Iga have selected you as their next target...

Masters of stealth, ninjitsu, both hand to hand and weapon combat, the ninja is an unprecedented threat on the battlefield, even a skilled samurai has to be cautious when fighting against a ninja!

Armed with ninjato, kamas, sais, throwing knives, tekko-kagi, even shuriken and a grappling hook, they use a variety of tools to overwhelm their opponents. Keeping their armour minimal, the ninja highly prioritises sheer offensive force to defeat their enemy.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: ゲット★スイートラブ (Get★Sweet Love)
description:
User summary
ゲット★スイートラブ (Get★Sweet Love) is a Japanese card game of match-making, but with somewhat adult themes and language.

In the game, players are dealt hand cards and draw and play more cards through the game. There are four female character cards dealt face up on the table, and the players are girl-chasers, competing to win the 'best' one. But which woman is best is only determined at the finish.

During the game, players play hand cards against their chosen targets. These attribute cards have positive values for virtues, and negative values for bad behaviour. Some cards are played face-up, some face-down, so there is a strong element of bluffing in the game. Some attribute cards alter the rules as they are played.

The game is very social and encourages table talk about the good or bad attributes. For example, during game play, players will talk up the virtues of one girl, and talk down the vices of another. "She is Born Rich! That's 6 points! I'm after her." "But she's also a Money-Waster. -8 points. Are you sure you want her? hehehe."
"You know what? This girl is so Gentle and Kind and worth 8 points!" "Poor you. You might have not known, but actually, she is a B*tch. -6 points!" "Oh no, she's a B*tch and also likes Kinky Sex? Oh my oh my..."

Players also put secret tokens on the female cards, to show their likes or dislikes. After the cards have been played, the players must 'Express Love' by simultaneously pointing at the woman they chose. If only player chooses one woman, they get her. If more than player points at one woman, the player with the highest token wins her.

Then all the attribute cards are flipped and the value of the different women is calculated. The player who picked the best woman, wins!

There are two Versions of the game, this original edition is themed on men trying to get the best girlfriend. There is another edition with the genders swapped, and the women are trying to get the best boyfriend - ゲット★スマートラブ (Get★Smart Love). Whilst the games are very similar, there are differences in the special cards which affect the gameplay. With both editions, you can combine and play an 8 player game.

The game name has a couple of twists. The English name can be abbreviated to Ge★S Love, which for Japanese readers equates to 'guess love', trying to guess which girl to choose. But the name ゲスラブ in Japanese, has the word ゲス in it, which is used in many vulgar or even obscene words in Japanese. So the publisher is deliberately signalling to buyers that the game is for adults but still sociable, since the girl-chasers will be nice to some girls, and not so nice to others. This version has gone through ten reprints in Japan and always draws a crowd at game shows, with its risque design (by Japanese standards).

Publisher's summary
グループ交際の中で相手の女性に様々な『属性』を付与し
最も点数の高い女性を恋人にした人が勝利。
通称ゲスラブ（Guess Love）

Get★Smart Loveと混ぜて1名～8名で遊ぶ事も可能。
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
publishers: しらたまゲームズ (Shiratama Games)

description:
The players have to drive their racing cars from start to finish. Movement is determined by a standard die (1-6) and a color die. The result of the color die decides which car is to be moved. So each player rolls for all players in the game. On illustrated spaces, events hinder or benefit the player. The first player to reach space 76 wins the game.

—user summary

The players have to move their token from start to finish. The token have holes in their center, which are used to move the token with a stick (lance). Movement is determined by 40 small cards with letters on them. The cards are used to form the words "Ruck" and "Zuck". The players start with 3 cards, lying open in front of them. Every turn the player draws a card. As soon as he can form one of the two words the player has to move his token accordingly. "Ruck" means forward and "Zuck" means backward. The number of spaces is equal to the number of cards used to form the word (2-4). If a player moves his token onto a green space, he may move forward to the next green space. A red space means the player has to move backward to the last red space.
The first player to reach the finish wins the game.

boardgame: Kill the Unicorns: The Underground Awakens
description:
Expand the Queendom with The Underground Awakens Expansion!

This expansion includes:
22 New Unicorn Cards
2 New Characters
16 Hunt Cards
16 Scheme Cards

Building a Better Monster pits players against each other as Mad Scientists competing to be the first to build a unique creature and bring it to life. When not acting as the Mad Scientist, you take on the role of Angry Villagers trying to work together to stop the current Mad Scientist's work.

The game plays using one standard 6-sided die to determine what twisted blueprints they’ve jotted down from fever-dreams and insane visions, as well as 6 custom Mad Scientist dice to determine what building blocks they’ve managed to collect from local cemeteries, mortuaries, hospitals, or prisons. Each scientist uses these materials to make their twisted schematics a reality. With the help of their faithful lab assistants and the occasional pack of ghouls at their summons, the madness is contagious. The Angry Villagers have their own set of 6 custom dice to determine their actions for the night. Will they storm the lab with a Pitchfork or Fire? Will they decide to cut off the Electricity instead? Maybe some will sleep through the night or decide to go Grave Robbing instead to use for their own creations.

—description from the publisher

In Paolo Tortellini players collect sets of foods to win. Each player is dealt 5 cards and draws one additional one each round, trying to get two sets of three cards each.

Essentially, it's a children's version of Rommé where you have to collect two sets of three cards each out of 8 sets of 4 cards.

Kagami is a one to four player solo or cooperative abstract game in which players attempt to mirror two sides of an eight by eight board or four quadrants for a more challenging experience and more players. The game is played without verbal communication. Players score based on the type and number of stones perfectly matched on the board.

boardgame: ゲット★スマートラブ (Get★Smart Love)
description:
User summary
ゲット★スマートラブ (Get★Smart Love) is a Japanese card game of match-making, but with somewhat adult themes and language.

In the game, players are dealt hand cards and draw and play more cards through the game. There are four male character cards dealt face up on the table, and the players are boy-chasers, competing to win the 'best' one. But which man is best is only determined at the finish.

During the game, players play hand cards against their chosen targets. These attribute cards have positive values for virtues, and negative values for bad behaviour. The game is very social and encourages table talk about the good or bad attributes.

Players also put secret tokens on the male cards, to show their likes or dislikes. After the cards have been played, the players must 'Express Love' by simultaneously pointing at the man they chose. Then all the attribute cards are flipped and the value of the different men is calculated. The player who picked the best man, wins!

There are two Versions of the game, this second edition is themed on women trying to get the best boyfriend. There is another edition with the genders swapped, and the men are trying to get the best girlfriend - ゲット★スイートラブ (Get★Sweet Love). So the publisher is deliberately signalling to buyers that the game is for adults but still sociable, since the girl-chasers will be nice to some girls, and not so nice to others. This version has gone through five reprints in Japan and always draws a crowd at game shows, with its risque design (by Japanese standards).

Publisher's summary
グループ交際の中で相手の男性に様々な『属性』を付与し
最も点数の高い男性を恋人にした人が勝利。
通称ゲスラブ（Guess Love）

Get★Sweet Loveと混ぜて1名～8名で遊ぶ事も可能。
publishers: しらたまゲームズ (Shiratama Games)

boardgame: クエストREクエスト (Quest Re quest)
description:
User summary
クエストREクエスト (Quest Re quest) is a Japanese card game based upon the common RPG dungeon scene, but here, the players are the village folk! Y'know, those "Welcom' to the town of so-and-so" or "A potion? That's some golds" talking NPC folks.

Since there is a dungeon nearby this town, you see many adventurers stepping by this town, every day. One day, one villager said "I bet that Warrior will  survive and get back.", followed by another saying "I don't think so, but I'd bet that Sorcerer will." In a small village with little amusements, this gambling quickly became the most popular game in town.

You are one of the villagers, betting on adventurers, which of them would come back alive. Of course you want to win, so don't forget to set traps to other adventurers!

One of the adventurers died? No worries, the town has a church and a Bishop with Resurrection skills. Problem solved!
Complaints? No way! Well, dead man tell no tales after all. And, no one wants to end up being ashes, by complaining to the church. Or do you?

March on to the dungeon!

Publisher's summary
舞台はダンジョン、プレイヤーは村人！
ダンジョンに挑む冒険者の誰が生き残るかに賭け、
他のプレイヤーの賭けている冒険者には罠の道を案内する。

相手は冒険者なのでたとえ倒れても大丈夫、教会完備です。
もちろん苦情も来たこともありません。
死人に口なしともいいますが、教会たてつくと灰にされかねません。
そうして今日もラットレースが開幕します！

さぁ、ゆきゆきて、ダンジョン！
publishers: しらたまゲームズ (Shiratama Games)

description:
The Storms mini-expansion adds new weather event cards for Snowbirds' Action deck, a collection of both storms and good weather to increase the game's challenge. These 9 cards offer trade-offs that modify the route, easing some aspects while complicating others. Players must work to play them effectively, maximizing benefits without succumbing to the dangers. It’s a risk/reward gamble that can boost your score or ground your flock early. Can your flock handle it?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Prognosticating Punch
description:
One of three cards for The Red Dragon Inn: 2016 SlugCrew Minor Reward. This card was given away if you have registered in SlugCrew and have done their events.

This drink allows you to look at any player's "Drink Me!" Pile and put it back in any order.

A second edition of this card is part of the The Red Dragon Inn Smorgasbox (Collector's Edition).

boardgame: The Pursuit of Happiness: KS Promos 2
description:
A bundle of mini-expansions and extra cards that were available during the Kickstarter for The Pursuit of Happiness: Community. A wooden Palm Tree first player marker was also included.

Friends Cards (10): A mini-expansion, Friend cards are placed between players and provide an action with potential end game bonus for one of the two players on each side of the card.

Ongoing Life Goals (3): Life goals checked at the end of each adult round.

Pets (8): Pets are special Item - Activity cards and can be combined with the 10 Pets from the base game KS Promos.

Stand-Alone Jobs (3): Consists of 3 new jobs that are shuffled in with the standard job cards and can be combined with the 3 Stand-Alone Jobs from the base game KS Promos.

There were also bonus cards of the following types:
Child Trait (2), Item - Activity (10), Partners (3), Projects (9).
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Golden Stout
description:
One of three cards for The Red Dragon Inn: 2016 SlugCrew Minor Reward. This card was given away if you have registered in SlugCrew and have done their events.

This drink forces you to pay 1 gold to the inn.

A second edition of this card is part of the The Red Dragon Inn Smorgasbox (Collector's Edition).

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Timewarp Tonic
description:
One of three cards for The Red Dragon Inn: 2016 SlugCrew Minor Reward. This card was given away if you have registered in SlugCrew and have done their events.

This drink brings you 3 alcohol content and an additional turn after the "Drink Me!" phase.

A second edition of this card is part of the The Red Dragon Inn Smorgasbox (all editions).

Lost My Mummy is a two player, set collection game. You are racing your opponent to find your mummies by collecting information about them. This could be the canopic jar, the embalming material, the amulet, or even a death wish. This information is depicted on the cards and these are what you collect over the course of the game.

However, the main dynamic of the game are the dice. These are all bespoke dice that slow up an opponent or speed you up in your race to collect the cards that you need. They have low, medium or high numbers on them and this determines the range of choice that you have to select cards on your turn. Once found, you can then collect the reward for your found Mummy. The player with the most reward money, wins!

This family-friendly game plays at a number of levels, from simple enjoyable gameplay to more complex strategies to win! Over time, players develop a better understanding about how to find their mummies in the quickest time.

The artwork is very detailed and allows for children to really explore the area of Egyptian mummification. They are actively learning about a period of history, without actually realising the information is going in! This is the power of this little card game.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Hair of the Dwarf
description:
This card came was the participation promo of The Red Dragon Inn Organised Play Season 2 (2016).

Hair of the Dwarf brings 2 alcohol contents and then lose 1 fortitude or discard 2 hand cards. Furthermore, it can't be splitted by any card.

The Organized Play Kits each contained 40 copies of this card (20 for each tournament) and 6 copies of Whitehawk’s Private Reserve (3 for each tournament).

A second edition of this card is part of the The Red Dragon Inn Smorgasbox (Collector's Edition).

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Whitehawk's Private Reserve
description:
This card is the premium promo of The Red Dragon Inn Organised Play Season 2 (2016).

It brings 2 alcohol contents to the user and 1 alcohol content to other players.

The Organized Play Kits each contained 6 copies of this card (3 for each tournament) and 40 copies of Hair of the Dwarf (20 for each tournament).

A second edition of this card is part of the The Red Dragon Inn Smorgasbox (Collector's Edition).

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Nitrel Glycerin
description:
This card was the participation promo of The Red Dragon Inn Organised Play Season 3 (2017).

It reduces 2 fortitude points and also discard 2 hand cards, then discard 2 drink cards at top of card user's "Drink Me!" Pile.

The Organized Play Kits each contained 40 copies of this card (20 for each tournament) and 6 of the Hellspawn Stout (3 for each tournament).

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Hellspawn Stout
description:
This card was the premium promo of The Red Dragon Inn Organised Play Season 3 (2017); it also appears in The Red Dragon Inn: Allies – Evil Pooky.

It makes a choose at the start: discard this card and then use the top card of the drink deck; reduce 3 fortitude points and take any card from character deck to hand card deck.

The Organized Play Kits each contained 6 copies of this card (3 for each tournament) and 40 of the Nitrel Glycerin (20 for each tournament).

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Blud Light
description:
It was the promotional card in Gen Con over 2017 and 2018 convention season. Later released as part of the Red Dragon Inn 10 Promo Pack 1.

It discards a hand card, then reduces 1 fortitude. A vampire character player gains 1 alcohol content instead of this fortitude loss.

The BIG Match in a Tiny Tin!

This is a 2-player card and dice game recreating the ﬂow and pace of a football/soccer match between two teams (Red and Blue), and is easily playable within 15-20 mins. It was originally designed to fit inside a standard Alto Mint Tin.

Using two standard ten-sided dice (2d10), this cracking little game helps to recreate all the ﬂow and jargon of a real football match, with substitutions, injuries, free kicks, corners, penalties, last-ditch saves, formation changes, players getting sent oﬀ and own goals too!

Players compare team strengths in three outfield areas (Defence, Midfield, and Attack) to determine a basic modifier to the Target/Progress Roll and then roll the dice to try and move the ball between their outfield areas in an eﬀort to score goals.

Included are playable Bonus Action Cards for each team, Event Cards that are drawn every time a Progress Roll fails (allowing formation changes, introducing injuries, providing second chances, adding injury time and tactical changes, etc), and an Xtreme Event deck that allows for those extra special moments when things go amazingly good, or disastrously bad!

If you download and play this game, please do leave any comments/feedback about your experiences — several players have asked if I'm planning a larger, full-card version, with more options and events, varied time segments and tighter controls, and this is certainly something I might be taking a look at over the next few months... Others have asked if I'm planning any expansions with real football player and team names, and to be honest I'd encourage anyone who wants to do this to freely supply replacement team/player ratings if they prefer. Better yet, upload and share them here!

Thanks in advance for taking the time to download and try it!

If you get to play it, please do rate it and add a comment -- all feedback is appreciated.

As a Roman Trader, it's your job to ensure you have the best and most prestigious collection of goods available for purchase...

Roman Trader is a straight set-collecting card game with a couple of novel twists, not least of which is the fact that the goods you're trying to collect are 'open' to other players — they may know what goods you appear to be collecting, but they won't know if you're putting together a specialist collection of varied goods from a particular country/principality!

The ability to collect from the discard pile and effectively set the game going backwards is another twist, and there's also a selection of special cards available to buy that allow you to increase your Warehouse space (hand-size), Plunder goods from other players, Smuggle goods long thought lost into your warehouse, Increase your Market share of selected goods, or Invest in the collections of other players!

Wine Collector is a visually dynamic tile-laying game in which you must try and satisfy the whims and wants of a selection of fussy Wine Collectors by finding the relevant wine in your cellar (represented by bottle/label parts), and putting together the relevant wine bottle from your collection of tiles...

Limits on available Grape varieties, Flavours, and Nuances will ensure it's not an easy task. Do you push-your-luck and hold out for 'the whole caboodle', or cut your losses and deliver a sub-standard product...?  It's your call, but when the last Wine Collector has left the building, it's game over!

Each round, players take it in turns to either draw a card from one of three decks (either a new one from the top, or the last previously discarded tile) or can choose to satisfy a Wine Collector's demands and 'claim' the relevant Income from that Collector. Collectors will pay well if all their demands are satisfied, or not-so-much if you can only meet some of their expectations...

There are three tile decks:

Grapes are double-sided tiles with a particular grape or wine variety on each side. These are placed in front of players when they're drawn, but each player may only have a maximum of four  grapes/varieties in front of them at any one time, but may choose to draw additional Grape tiles and discard down if necessary to ensure they do not exceed this limit. Grape cards will need to be given up for each Wine Collector claimed.

Flavours are tiles that have two representative flavours on them, and they can be utilised one way or the other by rotating the tile through 180 degrees. These are kept in a player's hand until used to claim a Wine Collector.

Finally, Nuances are tiles that have three descriptors on them, and can be used to satisfy Collector demands either in part or whole, depending on how they are used and which descriptors are covered by other tiles when making a claim. These tiles are also kept in a player's hand until used.

A player's hand size is limited to just six tiles/cards in total (excl. Grapes/Varieties which are kept on the table in front of them), and you must juggle these as best you can to make a call on what tiles are worth holding onto, and which ones can be safely discarded...

—description from the publisher

A children's deduction game in the Mastermind tradition.  The goal is to correctly guess the colour and type of three hidden fruits.  A player's guesses are limited by a hangman-like monkey puzzle.  Less accurate guesses use up more turns.

Chopply is a Mark 'Chopper' Read themed board game which uses the mechanics of a simplified version of Monopoly.
The game comes with an interactive DVD of Heath Franklin playing Chopper and is activated when landing on certain squares(functioning similar to Chance and Community Chest cards).
Like Monopoly the game is won when other players go bankrupt(which is significantly harder to achieve due to no house/hotel options). A 2 player game is most likely to take days to determine a winner.

—user summary

Clock Solitaire is a card game with a traditional deck of 52 cards. The game is called Clock Solitaire, because twelve piles of cards are arranged like a clock face around a 13th one in he middle. The object of the game is to complete all other four-of-a-kind before the four kings are completed.

The cards are dealt face down into 13 piles, twelve in a circle around the last one in the middle. The piles are numbered 1 to 12 as on a clock face with pile 13 in the middle.

The top card of the 13 pile is revealed. Play the card under the corresponding pile. Ace under the 1 pile, 2 under 2, and so on. Faces are played Jacks under 11, Queens under 12, and King under 13. Then reveal the top card of this pile and play it as described above. If there is no more the face down card on this pile continue with the next face down card in clockwise order.

The game is won, if all piles become face up piles of four-of-a-kind. The game is lost, if the fourth king is revealed before all other cards are played face up.

The Universe of Stellar Leap is expanding to all new frontiers, with resource focused planets, diverse asteroids, challenging traits, and more! Stellar Leap: Frontiers brings in new Planets, Asteroids, Events, and Traits that you can add to the base game for all new experiences. Solar System Completion Bonuses are introduced and allow players to significantly change Solar Systems how they see fit.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Balck Friday: The Tatsinskaya Raid, December 1942
description:
Wait a minute. Don't we mean BLACK Friday?

Nope. It's "Balck Friday," as in German General Hermann Balck. In the winter of 1942, his crack 11th Panzer Division fought an amazing series of battles along the Chir River in response to the Soviet surprise attacks that surrounded Stalingrad. It has been called one of the finest divisional actions of all time.

Bottom line, a single Panzer division, fighting one day after another (frequently after overnight marches) pretty much destroyed an entire Soviet tank army. And more.

But it's a virtually unknown story, as the disaster of Stalingrad and Manstein's relief effort (our own Wintergewitter game) get all the headlines. It may be hard to imagine, but minus Balck's extraordinary fire-brigade work, Stalingrad would have been much, much worse!

On Christmas Day (a Friday), Balck and his units were called upon once again. They had to stop a Soviet probe deep behind the porous German lines, aimed at knocking out the airfield at Tatsinkaya, which was supporting the Stalingrad Pocket. ATO spotlights Balck's amazing efforts with a new, unpublished and complete game.

With 100 die cut counters, a full color 8.5" x 11" map and just 4 pages of rules, you'll be able to quickly explore this one remarkable exploit during a very "dark December" for the Axis.

—description from the publisher

Scale: ca. 0.667 miles/hex, 8 hours/turn (from Jay Murphy's video posted on BGG on 10Jan2025: https://boardgamegeek.com/video/532153/balck-friday-the-tatsinskaya-raid-december-1942/balck-friday-intro ).
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
English:
In November 1443, George Kastrioti Skanderbeg, at the head of 300 warriors, abandons the Ottoman army during the Battle of Niš. This is the beginning of an epic quarter-century struggle for freedom, where the principalities of Albania become the shield of the old continent against the incursions of the Ottoman Empire.

Be a part of this historical, strategic board game. Relive this age whose greatness has echoed through the centuries. The age of Skanderbeg!

The focus of this board game is precisely this period in history, filled with the struggles of the empires, kingdoms, and principalities of that time to politically survive and thrive.

Command the economic and military forces of a country. Display your skills as a brilliant strategist by planning moves and conflicts in different territories. Take over castles via diplomacy or strength, which will ensure your victory against your rivals. Change the course of history in just a short time!

We’ve tried to faithfully portray the facts and historical characters of that period without influencing the balance of the game, but have also tried to allow enough freedom for different fun strategies.

It is our hope and aspiration as creators of the game to bring about a friendly atmosphere, because we believe that games serve to bond us with each other.

Shqip (Albanian):
Në nëntor të vitit 1443, Gjergj Kastrioti Skënderbeu në krye të 300 luftëtarëve, e lë ushtrinë otomane në mëshirë të fatit gjatë betejës së Nishit. Ky është fillimi i një epopeje çerek shekullore ku principata e arbërit i bëhet mburojë kontinentit të vjetër.

Bëhuni pjesë e kësaj loje historiko-strategjike, të epokës së artë, madheshtia e së cilës jehoi ndër shekuj. Epokës së Skënderbeut!

Fokusi i kësaj loje strategjike është pikërisht kjo periudhë e historisë, e mbushur me përpjekjet e principatës së arbërit, perandorisë Otomane, mbretërisë së Hungarisë dhe republikës së Venecias për të mbijetuar dhe përparuar politikisht.

Komando ekonominë dhe forcat ushtarake të një vendi. Shpalos vlerat e tua si një strateg i shkëlqyer duke planifikuar lëvizjet dhe konfliktet në territore të ndryshme.

Zotëro me diplomaci ose me forcë kështjella, që do të sigurojnë fitoren kundër rivalëve. Ndrysho kursin e historisë në një kohë të shkurtër!

boardgame: Bratz: C.I.Y. Shoppe Merch Master Game
description:
Express your Bratz spirit with the "C.I.Y. Shoppe Merch Master".  In this Super-Fab game, explore your inner-merchandising diva. Play 3 exciting mini-games: Charades, The Rathers and Trend Zone to collect everything you need to open up your C.I.Y. Shoppe.

—description from the publisher

In this roll-n-move game you collect items and money as you move your Bratz around the board, sometimes encountering mini-games that help you meet the requirements of your hidden goal.

—user summary

boardgame: Sails of Glory Ship Pack: Duc de Duras 1765 / Dauphin 1766
description:
The Duc de Duras was a merchant ship which regularly sailed half way around the world on behalf of the French East India Company. Her design allowed her to be quickly transformed into a warship in case of necessity to support the navy. When the French East India Company dissolved, all its ships were “adopted” by the French Navy, including the Duc the Duras. Purchased by King Louis XVI of France in early 1779, her size and new armament made her the equivalent of a 50–gun 4th rate ship of the line.

This ship pack can also be used to represent the French ex–merchant ship “Dauphin 1766 (1770)”.

Ship pack contains a new ship with base, ship card, ship log, and specific deck of maneuver cards.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Sails of Glory Ship Pack: Bertin 1761 / Berryer 1759
description:
The Bertin was an East Indiaman used for long–distance trading during the eighteenth century. She was later converted into a ship of the line, taking advantage of her design, already suitable for a warship. When, in 1770, she was commissioned into the French Marine Royale, under King Louis XVI, her armament was doubled – Bertin became a fourth rate with 56 guns and a crew of more than 300 men.

This ship pack can also be used to represent the French ex–merchant ship “Berryer 1759(1770)”.

Ship pack contains a new ship with base, ship card, ship log, and specific deck of maneuver cards.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Sails of Glory Ship Pack: Bonhomme Richard 1779 / Bonhomme Richard
description:
Originally named “Duc de Duras”, this ship was loaned by the French Crown to the newborn American Continental Navy, to be placed under the command of John Paul Jones; she was renamed “Bonhomme Richard”, in honor of Benjamin Franklin. During her cruise against the British, in the Battle of Flamborough Head, the Bonhomme Richard met her destiny fighting gloriously against the British Frigate HMS Serapis. Even if his ship was seriously damaged, John Paul Jones won the battle, but in the end lost his ship, which sunk in the aftermath of the battle.

This ship pack can also be used to represent another version of the “Bonhomme Richard 1779” with an alternative, possible armament.

Ship pack contains a new ship with base, ship card, ship log, and specific deck of maneuver cards.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Sails of Glory: Ship Pack – HMS Hamadryad 1797 / HMS Mahonesa 1796
description:
The HMS Hamadryad, before her capture, was named “Ninfa” — a 36–gun Spanish frigate built at Mahon, on the island of Minorca. After her capture, Ninfa was taken to Gibraltar where she was commissioned under Thomas Elphinstone as HMS Hamadryad. After capturing two privateers, her career as an English ship was a short one, as she sank whilst trying to shelter from a storm in Algiers Bay, on 25 December 1797.

This ship pack can also be used to represent the British Mahonesa class frigate “HMS Mahonesa 1796”.

Ship pack contains a new ship with base, ship card, ship log, and specific deck of maneuver cards.

—description from the publisher
